q_tail.question: Does the hindwing have a tail?
q_tail.hint: Check the posterior margin of the hindwing for a projection. Most Arhopala tails are at vein 2.

choices.label: Yes — hindwing is tailed
No — hindwing is tailless

q_tailed_vein3.question: Is the tail located at vein 3 and short?

question_link.text: tail located at vein 3

q_tailed_vein3.hint: In the vast majority of Arhopala the tail (when present) is at vein 2. Only 2 species in the entire genus have a tail at vein 3 — both in the abseus group: [A. abseus abseus](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=333427&preferred_place_id=6734) (small, deep violet-blue male) and [A. anella](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1294316&preferred_place_id=6734) (larger, very wide fuscous borders). The vein 3 tail is one vein closer to the forewing than usual, and is distinctively short and white-tipped. Also check for a spot at the base of hindwing space 1a.

choices.label: Yes — short white-tipped tail at vein 3 (only 3 spp. in entire genus)
No — tail at the usual vein 2 (typical position)

q_tailed_long_tail.question: Is the tail very long and thread-like — approximately 5 mm or longer?
q_tailed_long_tail.hint: Species with tails clearly reaching 5 mm or longer belong to three groups: [A. overdijkinki](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1390509&preferred_place_id=6734) (eumolphus group, ~5 mm), [A. aedias](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=498179&preferred_place_id=6734) (aedias group, ~6 mm), and the camdeo subgroup species [A. opalina](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=994269&preferred_place_id=6734), [A. opalina azata](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=994269&preferred_place_id=6734), [A. camdana camdana](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+camdana), [A. semperi russelli](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1148362&preferred_place_id=6734), and [A. dispar pendleburyi](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+dispar) (all ~4–6 mm — the upper end of this range clears 5 mm). [A. corinda](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=793494&preferred_place_id=6734) has a moderate tail (~3.5 mm); all other tailed species are ≤4 mm. Answer Yes only if the tail clearly exceeds 5 mm; answer No for tails up to ~4 mm.

choices.label: Yes — tail very long, approximately 5 mm or longer (eumolphus or aedias group)
No — tail moderate or typical for Arhopala (up to ~4 mm)
Cannot determine — tail length unclear or broken

q_ijanensis_hw_streak.question: Is there a whitish streak on the hindwing underside running from the base of the dorsum to the apex?
q_ijanensis_hw_streak.hint: Quick diagnostic for [A. ijanensis](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1555627&preferred_place_id=6734). This species has a distinctive whitish streak on the HW underside running from the base of the dorsum all the way to the apex, with a notably long HW cell (~half wing length or longer). If this streak is visible, the identification is [A. ijanensis](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1555627&preferred_place_id=6734). If absent, continue with the key.

choices.label: Yes — whitish streak present on HW underside from dorsal base to apex
No — no whitish streak

q_tailed_epimuta.question: On the hindwing underside, is the postdiscal spot in space 6 positioned roughly midway between the postdiscal spot in space 5 and the end-cell bar?

question_link.text: postdiscal spot in space 6

q_tailed_epimuta.hint:
Two groups have spot 6 displaced above spot 5’s level toward the end-cell bar: the epimuta group ([A. atosia](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=498181&preferred_place_id=6734), [A. lurida](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=475784&preferred_place_id=6734)) and the alitaeus group (including [A. pseudomuta](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=498185&preferred_place_id=6734) and [A. alitaeus](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=528441&preferred_place_id=6734)). The degree of displacement differs: in the epimuta group it is modest and spot 6 may appear close to spot 5’s level in some views; in the alitaeus group it is more pronounced — spot 6 is clearly above spot 5’s level and, in both pseudomuta and alitaeus, actually touches/overlaps the end-cell bar. Answer Yes for any upward displacement, even up to a full overlap, AS LONG AS the FW space 11 spot (checked next) or markings still point to the alitaeus group — don't be misled into the third 'No' option below just because spot 6 touches the bar. If Yes, a spot in FW space 11 will be checked next to tell pseudomuta/alitaeus apart from the epimuta group.

Answer No in all of these cases:
• Spot 6 is at the same level as spot 5 with no upward displacement (most tailed species). Select the first No option.
• Spot 6 is displaced toward the end-cell bar but clearly less than midway — the gap between spot 6 and the bar is distinctly wider than the displacement from the postdiscal row (e.g., [A. silhetensis adorea](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=498186&preferred_place_id=6734): spots 6 and 7 are oblique, outer edge of spot 6 well inside inner edge of spot 5, but spot 6 does not approach midway).
• Spot 6 is displaced well past midway — touching or overlapping the end-cell bar, with spots 5, 6 and 7 widely out of line — in groups OTHER than alitaeus/pseudomuta ([A. agrata](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=859032&preferred_place_id=6734) and related species; also the eumolphus group: spot 6 inner edge is in line with or inside the inner edge of spot 7). Select the second No option.

choices.label: Yes — spot 6 is displaced above spot 5's level toward the end-cell bar (displacement may be subtle in the epimuta group — any upward shift counts)
No — spot 6 not displaced; in the postdiscal row at the same level as spot 5, with no upward shift toward the end-cell bar
No — spot 6 displaced past midway; touching or overlapping the end-cell bar; spots 5, 6 and 7 widely out of line

q_epimuta_alitaeus_split.question: Is there a central spot in forewing underside space 11?

question_link.text: central spot in forewing underside space 11

q_epimuta_alitaeus_split.hint: A central spot in FW space 11 (above the cell) identifies the alitaeus group, which shares the 'midway spot 6' character with the epimuta group. [A. pseudomuta](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=498185&preferred_place_id=6734) and other alitaeus-group members have this spot; the epimuta group ([A. atosia](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=498181&preferred_place_id=6734), [A. lurida](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=475784&preferred_place_id=6734)) does not. If visible, this character is decisive.

choices.label: Yes — central spot present in FW space 11
No — no central spot in FW space 11

q_tailed_long_dark.question: On the forewing underside, is the basal half of space 1b completely darkened?
q_tailed_long_dark.hint: The corinda group is immediately identified by the basal half of FW underside space 1b being completely darkened, bounded outwardly by a pale line. No other tailed Arhopala with a moderate tail (up to ~4 mm) shares this character. FW space 1b is often hidden under the hindwing in field shots — if not visible, select Cannot determine. Note: [A. corinda](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=793494&preferred_place_id=6734) also has a distinctly pointed FW apex and straight termen, which may help confirm the identification.

choices.label: Yes — basal half of FW underside space 1b completely darkened (corinda group)
No — basal half of FW space 1b not completely darkened
Cannot determine — FW space 1b obscured by hindwing

q_long_tail_length.question: What is the underside ground colour and overall appearance?
q_long_tail_length.hint: When FW space 1b and the HW spot arrangement are both unclear, use these field characters to separate the long-tailed species. [A. aedias](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=498179&preferred_place_id=6734) (Large Metallic Oakblue): ground colour hair brown; spots narrowly outlined in pale buff lines; HW spots 5–6–7 in echelon (spot 6 nearer to spot 5); large species (FW 20–29 mm); rounded FW apex; tail ~6 mm. [A. corinda](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=793494&preferred_place_id=6734): ground colour sometimes glazed with purple wash; large dark patch visible under FW cell when not obscured; FW apex distinctly pointed with straight termen; tail ~3.5 mm. [A. overdijkinki](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1390509&preferred_place_id=6734) (eumolphus group): hair brown underside; HW spot 6 directly below spot 7 (not in echelon); tail ~5 mm. Corinda subspecies: acestes (Malay Peninsula and Singapore), corestes (Langkawi). Aedias subspecies: agnis (standard); meritatas (Langkawi).

choices.label: Hair brown; HW spot 6 directly below spot 7 (not echelon); tail ~5 mm — A. overdijkinki
Hair brown; spots narrowly buff-outlined; HW spots 5–6–7 in echelon; large (FW 20–29 mm); rounded FW apex; tail ~6 mm — A. aedias
Underside sometimes with purple wash; dark patch under FW cell; pointed FW apex, straight termen; smaller (tail ~3.5 mm) — A. corinda
Cannot determine — none of these descriptions clearly match

q_long_tail_spot4.question: On the hindwing underside, is postdiscal spot 6 placed directly below spot 7 — not in echelon with spots 5 and 7?

question_link.text: not in echelon with spots 5 and 7

q_long_tail_spot4.hint: This separates [A. overdijkinki](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1390509&preferred_place_id=6734) (eumolphus group, tail ~5 mm) from the echelon-spot species. In [A. overdijkinki](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1390509&preferred_place_id=6734), HW postdiscal spot 6 sits directly below spot 7, breaking the diagonal row. Note: [A. eumolphus](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=498188&preferred_place_id=6734) also has spot 6 directly below spot 7, but its tail is only ~3 mm — if the tail is not clearly ~5 mm, go back and answer No at the previous question. In [A. aedias](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=498179&preferred_place_id=6734) (aedias group, tail ~6 mm) and [A. opalina](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=994269&preferred_place_id=6734) (camdeo group, tail 4–6 mm), spots 5, 6, and 7 are arranged in echelon — with spot 6 sitting closer to spot 5 than to spot 7. If echelon is confirmed, a follow-up question checks for the camdeo-group dark area under the FW cell.

choices.label: Yes — HW spot 6 directly below spot 7 (not in echelon with spots 5 and 7)
No — HW spots 5, 6 and 7 in echelon; spot 6 nearer to spot 5
Cannot determine — HW spot arrangement unclear

q_tailed_ganesa.question: Is this a small species with spots above the forewing cell and a spot at the base of forewing space 3?

question_link.text: spots above the forewing cell

q_tailed_ganesa.hint: The ganesa group is characteristically small, with spots visible above the FW cell on the underside, a spot at the base of FW space 3, and short tails. The spot at the base of FW space 3 is usually hidden under the hindwing in resting photographs — use small size and spots above the FW cell as the primary characters; if both are clearly present, answer Yes. Three of the five Peninsular species ([A. ammon](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=506221&preferred_place_id=6734), [A. ammonides](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=470744&preferred_place_id=6734), [A. elizabethae](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+elizabethae)) also show a conspicuously whitened gap between the outer and central spots in HW space 7.

choices.label: Yes — small; spots above FW cell; spot at base of FW space 3 (if visible)
No

q_tailed_cell_silver.question: Are the cell spots on the forewing underside outlined in greenish silver?

question_link.text: cell spots on the forewing underside

q_tailed_cell_silver.hint: Examine the underside forewing cell. [A. centaurus](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=146903&preferred_place_id=6734) has distinctive greenish-silver lines edging the cell markings — not iridescence but a true metallic outline unique to this species in Peninsular Malaysia. If present, this is decisive: no further questions needed. Non-Peninsular relatives with similar markings ([A. araxes](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+araxes), Sumatra–Sulawesi; [A. amantes](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+amantes), S. Myanmar) are not recorded from the Malay Peninsula.

choices.label: Yes — greenish silver outlines on FW cell spots
No — no silver outlines
Cannot determine — FW cell not clearly visible

q_tailed_dark_area.question: Is there a restricted dark patch in FW space 1b (under the forewing cell), bounded outwardly by a pale line?
q_tailed_dark_area.hint: The camdeo group has a restricted dark patch in FW underside space 1b with a clearly defined pale line running outwardly across it — this is a more localised marking than the complete basal-half darkening of the corinda group (already excluded). In field photographs this area is often hidden under the hindwing — answer No if the patch is absent or if the area is not visible.

choices.label: Yes — restricted dark patch present in FW space 1b with pale bordering line (camdeo group)
No — no dark patch present in FW space 1b
Cannot determine — FW space 1b obscured by hindwing

q_camdeo_sub.question: What does the hindwing underside look like?
q_camdeo_sub.hint: Check the overall ground colour and look for an extra spot in the middle of HW space 8. The position of HW postdiscal spot 6 relative to spot 5 and the end-cell bar is also diagnostic: in this group, spot 6 sits clearly nearer to spot 5. If spot 6 appears midway between spot 5 and the end-cell bar — typical of the epimuta and agesilaus groups — select the last option.

choices.label: Crimson-brown ground colour with markings outlined in white striae; spot at the base of HW space 6
Purple-brown ground colour with whitish striae; extra spot visible mid-space 8 adjoining the central spot in space 7
Brown or grey-brown underside; HW spot 6 clearly nearer to spot 5 than to the end-cell bar — no crimson colouring and no extra spot in space 8
HW spot 6 appears midway between spot 5 and the end-cell bar, or the underside does not match any of the above — may not be the right group

q_tailed_anthelus.question: Is the forewing postdiscal band only partially dislocated at vein 4, with a spot at the base of FW space 10 but NO spot at the base of HW space 6?

question_link.text: spot at the base of FW space 10

q_tailed_anthelus.hint: The anthelus group (2 spp.) is defined by partial dislocation of the FW postdiscal band at vein 4, a spot at the base of FW space 10, and the absence of a spot at the base of HW space 6. [A. anthelus grahami](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=149211&preferred_place_id=6734): ≥3 spots in FW space 11 and 2 subbasal spots in HW space 8; FW 28 mm. [A. achelous achelous](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=940963&preferred_place_id=6734): no spots in FW space 11 and only 1 HW space 8 spot; FW 22 mm; HW spot 6 noticeably smaller than spot 7 — roughly a quarter its size; strong purple tinge on underside (nominate); subspecies malu (Kedawi) lacks most of the purple tinge.

choices.label: Yes — FW band partially dislocated at vein 4; spot at base of FW space 10; no spot at base of HW space 6
No

q_tailed_macular.question: Are the underside markings macular — composed of separated lines and dots rather than continuous solid bands?
q_tailed_macular.hint: The democritus group has markings that are broken up into isolated dots and short lines, not forming a continuous postdiscal band. The HW postdiscal band is also widely dislocated at vein 2.

choices.label: Yes — macular markings (dots/lines); HW postdiscal band widely dislocated at vein 2
No — markings form more continuous bands

q_alesia_ground_colour.question: Is the underside ground colour pale grey-brown — distinctly paler than the typical hair-brown Arhopala underside — with the hindwing spaces 4–5 spots and end-cell bar pale and low-contrast (each ringed by a black border, then a white outer ring), while the forewing postdiscal band remains strongly dark and contrasts sharply against the pale ground?
q_alesia_ground_colour.hint: (per field-photo review) Distinctive early-exit character for [A. alesia sacharja](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+alesia): underside ground colour pale grey-brown, not the typical hair-brown. HW postdiscal spots in spaces 4–5 and the end-cell bar are very pale, close to the ground colour with little contrast, each ringed by a black border and then a white outer ring — yet the FW postdiscal band is almost entirely dark/black, strongly contrasting against the pale ground. Its only close rival, [A. barami penanga](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=958932&preferred_place_id=6734), is typical hair-brown underside with solid spots darker than the ground colour — answer No for it. If unsure, answer No and continue; the spot-6/size characters ahead still separate the two.

choices.label: Yes — pale grey-brown ground colour; HW spaces 4–5 spots and end-cell bar pale, ringed black then white, low-contrast; FW band strongly dark by contrast
No — typical hair-brown underside; spots solid, darker than the ground colour

q_alaconia_wedge_tailed.question: Among tailed species: in hindwing underside spaces 4 and 5, are the submarginal spots prominently black and wedge-shaped (∧), with the postdiscal spots large and edged by a thick, pale border?
q_alaconia_wedge_tailed.hint: (per field-photo review) This combination — bold, blackish wedge-shaped (∧) submarginal spots in HW spaces 4–5, paired with large postdiscal spots bordered by a conspicuously thick pale margin — is unique to the tailed f. kempi form of [Arhopala alaconia media](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=880741&preferred_place_id=6734) among Malaysian Arhopala and is usually clear even in average photos. If present, you can identify the species directly here without checking HW spot 6 position/shape.

choices.label: Yes — HW spaces 4–5 submarginal spots black, wedge-shaped (∧); postdiscal spots large with a thick pale border
No — HW spaces 4–5 submarginal spots not wedge-shaped, or postdiscal spots not thickly pale-bordered

q_tailed_alitaeus.question: Is there a central spot in forewing underside space 11?

question_link.text: central spot in forewing underside space 11

q_tailed_alitaeus.hint: The alitaeus group is defined by having a central spot in FW space 11 (above the cell). Note: the anthelus group has ≥3 spots in space 11 but was already checked. The alitaeus group also has a distinctive right-angled male aedeagus (requires dissection).

choices.label: Yes — central spot present in FW space 11
No — no central spot in FW space 11

q_tailed_cleander.question: Does the hindwing have a projecting tornal lobe (a bulge at the anal angle)?
q_tailed_cleander.hint: The cleander group has a projecting tornal lobe (bulge at the anal angle of the hindwing) — its size and prominence vary by subgroup, from large and conspicuous (cleander subgroup) to smaller and less conspicuous (alea and agaba subgroups). The next question narrows down the subgroup.

choices.label: Yes — tornal lobe present and projecting (size and prominence vary by subgroup — see next question)
No — tornal lobe absent or only weakly developed

q_cleander_sub.question: Which of these best matches the specimen's size, tornal lobe, and hindwing markings?
q_cleander_sub.hint: Agaba subgroup: HW postdiscal spot 5 contiguous or amalgamated with end-cell bar; HW band only PARTIALLY dislocated at vein 2 (unlike others). Cleander subgroup: tornal lobe large and clearly visible; underside plain hair brown; FW typically 22–26 mm (athada 22–23, cleander 23–24, silhetensis 23–26 mm). Alea subgroup: tornal lobe present but smaller and less prominent; FW typically 17–21 mm (phanda 17–19, selta/aroa/evansi 18–21, aurelia 19–20 mm); aurelia and evansi have a distinctive slatey purple or pinkish underside glaze.

choices.label: HW spot 5 contiguous/merged with end-cell bar; HW band partially dislocated at vein 2 (agaba subgroup)
Tornal lobe large and conspicuously projecting; underside plain hair brown; FW typically 22–26 mm (cleander subgroup)
Tornal lobe smaller and less prominent; FW typically 17–21 mm; some with slatey purple or pinkish underside (alea subgroup)

q_tailed_agrata.question: What is the shape of the hindwing underside central cell spot, and are there specific absences on the forewing underside?

question_link.text: hindwing underside central cell spot

q_tailed_agrata.hint: The agrata group has two subgroups: ace (with a band-like cell spot across the whole HW cell) and agrata (lacking a FW postdiscal spot in space 9 and lacking a costal spot in space 10).

choices.label: HW central cell spot is band-like, stretching entirely across the cell (ace subgroup)
FW lacks postdiscal spot in space 9; no costal spot in FW space 10 (agrata subgroup)
Neither of these

q_tailed_spot_arrangement.question: What best describes the forewing postdiscal spot 4 and the hindwing postdiscal spot 6?

question_link.text: postdiscal spot 4

q_tailed_spot_arrangement.hint: HW spot 6 shape is the decisive character here. If spot 6 is outwardly sinuous (S-curved), choose the sinuous option regardless of FW spot 4 position — [A. buddha](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=885913&preferred_place_id=6734) (rama group) has both FW spot 4 out of line AND sinuous spot 6, so it belongs to the sinuous choice. The 'FW spot 4 out of line' option applies only when spot 6 is not sinuous (aurea and eumolphus groups).

choices.label: FW spot 4 out of line with others; HW spot 6 placed directly below spot 7 (spot 6 not sinuous)
FW spot 4 in continuation with spots 5 and 6; HW spot 6 rounded and more or less separated
HW spot 6 is outwardly sinuous; costal spot present in FW space 10 (select this even if FW spot 4 is also out of line)
FW postdiscal band is continuous and unbroken; no costal spot in FW space 10
HW postdiscal spots 5, 6, and 7 overlap with their centres forming a straight line (echelon)

q_tailed_agelastus_check.question: What is the shape and position of the hindwing postdiscal spot 6, and are the inner submarginal spots notably enlarged and darkened?

question_link.text: position of the hindwing postdiscal spot 6

q_tailed_agelastus_check.hint: Two tailed exceptions occur within the otherwise-tailless agelastus group, distinguished chiefly by HW spot 6: [A. barami penanga](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=958932&preferred_place_id=6734) AND [A. alesia sacharja](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+alesia) both have spot 6 outwardly convex, widely displaced toward (often touching/overlapping) the end-cell bar, with spots 5–6–7 widely out of line and uniform (not enlarged) submarginal spots — field photos show these two are effectively identical here, despite older literature describing A. alesia sacharja's spot 6 as concave; the next question separates them by size and border width. The tailed f. kempi form of [A. alaconia media](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=880741&preferred_place_id=6734) is genuinely different: spot 6 outwardly concave or sinuous, positioned below spot 7 — in the normal postdiscal row, not displaced — paired with strikingly enlarged and darkened inner submarginal spots in HW spaces 4–5.

choices.label: Spot 6 outwardly convex ⌢; touches or overlaps the end-cell bar; spots 5, 6 and 7 widely out of line; submarginal spots uniform (not enlarged)
Spot 6 outwardly concave ⌣ or sinuous ∿, positioned below spot 7 (in the normal row, not displaced); inner submarginal spots in HW spaces 4–5 notably enlarged and darkened

q_tailed_barami_alesia.question: Is this a relatively large specimen, with a forewing length of approximately 22.0–23.0 mm?
q_tailed_barami_alesia.hint: [A. barami penanga](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=958932&preferred_place_id=6734) is relatively large for the group (FW 22.0–23.0 mm) and also has an exceptionally broad male upperside border (2.5 mm at tornus widening to 8.0 mm at costa, if the upperside is visible). [A. alesia sacharja](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+alesia) is smaller, with a more moderate upperside border (~3.0 mm at tornus, not reaching such an extreme width); it is also known from only a single historical Malaysian record (Langkawi, 1994), so default to A. barami penanga if size cannot be assessed.

choices.label: Yes — relatively large, FW ~22.0–23.0 mm
No — smaller
Cannot determine — size not assessable

q_tailed_aurea_check.question: Does the hindwing cell extend considerably longer than half the wing length, combined with an acute or concave FW apex?
q_tailed_aurea_check.hint: The aurea group ([A. aurea](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=429759&preferred_place_id=6734), [A. horsfieldi](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=888216&preferred_place_id=6734)) has an exceptionally long hindwing cell — considerably more than half the wing length. The FW apex character is sex-dependent: males have a rather acute apex with a termen that is straight or slightly concave (particularly below vein 5); females have rounded forewings. For female specimens, rely on HW cell length alone. Males of the aurea group are also shining green, whereas eumolphus males are blue or purple — but use the cell length as the primary character.

choices.label: Yes — HW cell considerably longer than half wing; FW apex acute or concave (male) / rounded (female)
No — HW cell moderate length (eumolphus or oenea group)

q_amphimuta_sub.question: Within the amphimuta group (echelon HW spots 5–7), which subgroup best fits the specimen?

question_link.text: echelon HW spots 5–7

q_amphimuta_sub.hint:
Four subgroups. Muta: spots more quadrate/banded, FW space 2 stepped toward termen. Kurzi and perimuta subgroups: HW postdiscal band fully dislocated at vein 2 — two clearly separate portions with a visible gap; a follow-up question separates [A. kurzi](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1149928&preferred_place_id=6734) from the perimuta subgroup ([A. perimuta](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=448212&preferred_place_id=6734), [A. metamuta](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1340245&preferred_place_id=6734), [A. antimuta](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=498184&preferred_place_id=6734)). Amphimuta (largest, 12 spp.): spots rounded, cilia often prolonged into a short tooth at vein 2.

If the FW space 2 spot position isn't visible (forewing underside not visible), choose 'Cannot determine' — [A. antimuta](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=498184&preferred_place_id=6734) (perimuta subgroup) is by far the most abundant species in this group and can still be recognized by its own characters (spot 6 positioned directly below spot 7, touching or overlapping the end-cell bar, with a thread-like male border), while [A. perimuta](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=448212&preferred_place_id=6734) and [A. metamuta](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1340245&preferred_place_id=6734) have their own distinctive HW colours.

choices.label: Spots more quadrate/banded (muta subgroup)
HW postdiscal band fully dislocated at vein 2 — two clearly separate portions with a visible gap (kurzi or perimuta subgroup)
Spots rounded; tailed — a distinct tail present at vein 2 (A. delta)
Spots rounded; tailless — cilia may be prolonged but no distinct tail at vein 2
Cannot determine — FW space 2 spot position not visible, so muta subgroup cannot be ruled out (check kurzi/perimuta species characters)

q_amphimuta_dislocated_split.question: On the hindwing underside, are postdiscal spots 4 and 5 in a precise, straight (vertical) alignment with each other, distinct from the broader 5-6-7 echelon?

question_link.text: 5-6-7 echelon

q_amphimuta_dislocated_split.hint:
[A. kurzi](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1149928&preferred_place_id=6734) (sole member of the kurzi subgroup, tailless with a very rounded HW tornus): spots 5, 6 and 7 are roughly in echelon, but spots 4 and 5 specifically line up in a tight, straight vertical alignment — a more reliable cue than the broader 5-6-7 echelon. The FW underside also has a spot in space 1b shifted inward, placed midway between the end-cell bar and the postdiscal spot in space 2.

In the perimuta subgroup — [A. antimuta](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=498184&preferred_place_id=6734) (by far the most abundant amphimuta-group species), [A. perimuta](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=448212&preferred_place_id=6734), and [A. metamuta](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1340245&preferred_place_id=6734) — spots 4 and 5 are NOT in this tight alignment. Instead, spot 6 is displaced well past the postdiscal row, positioned directly below spot 7 (its inner edge in line with or inside the inner edge of spot 7), typically touching or overlapping the end-cell bar.

If the FW underside basal cell spot is absent, this points to [A. avathina avathina](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=888416&preferred_place_id=6734) (amphimuta subgroup) instead — select that option below.

If neither alignment is clearly visible, choose 'Cannot determine' — [A. antimuta](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=498184&preferred_place_id=6734) is far more common than [A. kurzi](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1149928&preferred_place_id=6734), so the key will continue with the perimuta subgroup.

choices.label: Yes — HW spots 4 and 5 precisely aligned in a straight vertical line (A. kurzi)
No — spot 6 displaced well past the row, positioned directly below spot 7, touching or overlapping the end-cell bar (perimuta subgroup)
FW underside basal cell spot is absent (consider A. avathina avathina)
Cannot determine — spot alignment not clearly visible

q_tailless_fulla.question: Are the underside markings almost entirely suppressed — the postdiscal spots replaced by a faint, obscure streak rather than a series of distinct separate spots, with most other typical markings absent?
q_tailless_fulla.hint: [A. fulla intaca](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=880792&preferred_place_id=6734) is instantly recognisable by the near-total suppression of underside markings. In most Arhopala the underside shows a row of separate, distinct postdiscal spots and a small rectangular mark at the end of the forewing discal cell — in [A. fulla](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=880792&preferred_place_id=6734) both are absent or nearly so. The postdiscal spots on both wings are replaced by an obscure diffuse streak; the rectangular mark at the end of the forewing cell is completely absent (and may also be absent on the hindwing). Only a few faint spots remain near the base of the hindwing. [A. alica](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1495881&preferred_place_id=6734) — markings almost entirely absent with only a faint trace of HW postdiscal marking visible; no postdiscal streak. If the underside shows normal, distinct postdiscal spots and a visible mark at the cell end, answer No.

choices.label: Yes — postdiscal spots replaced by a faint obscure streak; markings almost entirely absent
Underside markings almost entirely absent — only a faint trace of HW postdiscal marking visible; no postdiscal streak or band (A. alica)
No — postdiscal spots distinct; typical markings present

q_tailless_inornata.question: Does the hindwing have a very rounded tornus, and are tornal green scales absent?

question_link.text: very rounded tornus, and are tornal green scales absent

q_tailless_inornata.hint: The inornata group has a distinctively rounded hindwing tornus (anal angle). Unlike many other tailless Arhopala, it lacks metallic green or brassy tornal scales.

choices.label: Yes — very rounded HW tornus; no tornal green scales
No

q_tailless_perimuta.question: Is there a prominent yellowish area in the centre of the hindwing underside, and are the underside markings generally obscure or ill-defined?
q_tailless_perimuta.hint: [A. perimuta regina](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=448212&preferred_place_id=6734) is one of the easiest tailless Arhopala to recognise: the hindwing underside has a striking central yellowish discal area (not white or grey), and the markings across both wings are generally obscure or ill-defined — ill-defined enough that precise spot positions can be hard to assess. It also completely lacks the metallic green or brassy tornal scales seen in most other tailless Arhopala. If the HW discal area is the usual whitish-grey and the markings are well-defined, continue.

choices.label: Yes — prominent yellowish HW central discal area; markings obscure or ill-defined; no tornal green scales
No — HW discal area not prominently yellowish; markings well-defined

q_tailless_hypomuta.question: Is there a small spot at the extreme base of hindwing space 6 on the underside?

question_link.text: small spot at the extreme base of hindwing space 6

q_tailless_hypomuta.hint: This spot sits right at the base of space 6, closer to the wing base than the normal postdiscal spot row. It distinguishes the hypomuta group from the similar amphimuta group in most Malayan specimens. Note: the spot is usually absent in Bornean and Sumatran material.

choices.label: Yes — small spot at extreme base of HW space 6
No

q_tailless_hypomuta_camdeo_check.question: Is the underside crimson-brown with markings outlined by white striae, with a restricted dark patch in FW space 1b bounded by a pale line?
q_tailless_hypomuta_camdeo_check.hint: The myrzala subgroup of the camdeo group ([A. myrzala](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=498177&preferred_place_id=6734), [A. myrzalina](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1564116&preferred_place_id=6734)) ALSO has a small spot at the base of HW space 6, which can be mistaken for the hypomuta-group character just checked. If the underside additionally shows the camdeo group's crimson-brown colouring with white striae and the diagnostic restricted dark patch in FW space 1b, this is the myrzala subgroup, not [A. hypomuta](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=887008&preferred_place_id=6734) — continue down that route instead.

choices.label: No — underside not crimson-brown, or no FW space-1b dark patch (true hypomuta-group character)
Yes — crimson-brown with white striae and FW space-1b dark patch (myrzala subgroup)

q_tailless_agesias.question: Are the underside spots prominent and rounded, with a central spot in FW space 11?

question_link.text: FW space 11

q_tailless_agesias.hint: The agesias group has large, rounded, prominent spots (not narrow bands). The key feature is a central spot in FW space 11 (above the cell). FW vein 9 is also noticeably shorter than usual in this group, but this can be hard to confirm from photos — the spot pattern alone is sufficient.

choices.label: Yes — prominent rounded spots; central FW spot in space 11
No

q_alaconia_wedge_tailless.question: Among tailless species: in hindwing underside spaces 4 and 5, are the submarginal spots prominently black and wedge-shaped (∧), with the postdiscal spots large and edged by a thick, pale border?
q_alaconia_wedge_tailless.hint: (per field-photo review) This combination — bold, blackish wedge-shaped (∧) submarginal spots in HW spaces 4–5, paired with large postdiscal spots bordered by a conspicuously thick pale margin — is unique to [Arhopala alaconia media](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=880741&preferred_place_id=6734) among Malaysian Arhopala and is usually clear even in average photos. If present, you can identify the species directly here without checking HW spot 6 position/shape.

choices.label: Yes — HW spaces 4–5 submarginal spots black, wedge-shaped (∧); postdiscal spots large with a thick pale border
No — HW spaces 4–5 submarginal spots not wedge-shaped, or postdiscal spots not thickly pale-bordered

q_tailless_epimuta.question: On the hindwing underside, what best describes the position of postdiscal spot 6 relative to the postdiscal row and the end-cell bar?

question_link.text: position of postdiscal spot 6 relative to the postdiscal row and the end-cell bar

q_tailless_epimuta.hint: If spot 6 looks borderline between two adjacent options, choose the more displaced one — e.g. choose 'Equidistant' rather than 'Not displaced / Slightly displaced'; choose 'Widely displaced' rather than 'Equidistant'.

choices.label: Not displaced / Slightly displaced — spot 6 roughly level with spot 5, or only minimally shifted inward; spots 5, 6 and 7 broadly in line
Equidistant — spot 6 roughly midway between spot 5 and the end-cell bar
Significantly displaced inward toward the discal cell; spot 6 clearly nearer to the end-cell bar than to spot 5, but not touching (e.g. A. labuana)
Spot 6 touching or overlapping the end-cell bar; or spots 5, 6 and 7 widely out of line / displaced
Cannot determine — HW spot 6 position unclear

q_tailless_epimuta_vein2.question: Is the HW postdiscal band only partially dislocated at vein 2 — the band is shifted but still connected, not fully separated into two distinct portions?
q_tailless_epimuta_vein2.hint: Both the agesilaus group ([A. agesilaus gesa](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1278725&preferred_place_id=6734), [A. avatha](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1062607&preferred_place_id=6734)) and the agelastus group ([A. wildeyana](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1294333&preferred_place_id=6734), [A. labuana](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+labuana), etc.) have the HW postdiscal band only partially dislocated at vein 2 — it shifts inward but the two portions remain connected. In the epimuta group and in [A. antimuta](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=498184&preferred_place_id=6734), [A. metamuta](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1340245&preferred_place_id=6734), and [A. perimuta](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=448212&preferred_place_id=6734), the band is fully dislocated at vein 2 — two clearly separate portions with a visible gap. Answer Yes if the band appears continuous or only slightly offset at vein 2; a follow-up question about the FW postdiscal band will then separate agelastus from agesilaus. Answer No if there is a clear gap.

choices.label: Yes — band only partially dislocated at vein 2 (shifted but connected)
No — band fully dislocated at vein 2 (two separate portions)

q_tailless_vein2_rama_check.question: Is hindwing postdiscal spot 6 outwardly sinuous or concave, with a costal spot present in forewing underside space 10, above the end-cell bar?
q_tailless_vein2_rama_check.hint: [A. arvina adalitas](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1294761&preferred_place_id=6734) (the only tailless rama-group species) also has spots 5, 6 and 7 widely out of line with HW spot 6 touching/overlapping the end-cell bar, which can land it on this branch — but its sinuous/concave spot 6 shape and FW costal spot in space 10 separate it from the muta, agelastus and agesilaus groups below.

choices.label: Yes — HW spot 6 outwardly sinuous or concave; costal spot in FW space 10 above the end-cell bar
No — spot 6 not sinuous/concave, or no FW costal spot in space 10

q_tailless_epimuta_tornal.question: Are green tornal scales present on the HW underside?
q_tailless_epimuta_tornal.hint: Look at the tornal angle of the HW underside (the lower inner corner). [A. perimuta regina](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=448212&preferred_place_id=6734) is immediately separated by the complete absence of green tornal scales, combined with a central yellowish discal area and obscure or almost-absent markings. If green tornal scales are present, continue.

choices.label: No — green tornal scales absent; HW has yellowish discal area; markings obscure or almost absent
Yes — green tornal scales present

q_tailless_epimuta_spot6_pos.question: Are HW postdiscal spots 5, 6 and 7 widely out of line — with the inner edge of spot 6 in line with or inside the inner edge of spot 7, and spot 6 touching or overlapping the end-cell bar?

question_link.text: widely out of line — with the inner edge of spot 6 in line with or inside the inner edge of spot 7, and spot 6 touching or overlapping the end-cell bar

q_tailless_epimuta_spot6_pos.hint: In [A. antimuta antimuta](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=498184&preferred_place_id=6734), spot 6 is displaced well past the postdiscal row — its inner edge reaches or passes the inner edge of spot 7, and it typically touches or overlaps the end-cell bar; spots 5, 6 and 7 are widely out of line. In [A. metamuta metamuta](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1340245&preferred_place_id=6734), spot 6 is only slightly displaced — nearer to spot 5 than to the end-cell bar; spots 5, 6 and 7 are broadly in line.

choices.label: Yes — spots 5, 6 and 7 widely out of line; inner edge of spot 6 in line with or inside inner edge of spot 7; spot 6 touches or overlaps end-cell bar
No — spots broadly in line; spot 6 only slightly displaced, nearer to spot 5

q_tailless_epimuta_midway.question: On the hindwing underside, what best describes the position of postdiscal spot 6 relative to the postdiscal row and the end-cell bar?

question_link.text: position of postdiscal spot 6 relative to the postdiscal row and the end-cell bar

q_tailless_epimuta_midway.hint: At this point only two groups remain: the epimuta group ([A. atosia](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=498181&preferred_place_id=6734), [A. lurida](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=475784&preferred_place_id=6734)) and [A. metamuta metamuta](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1340245&preferred_place_id=6734). Both have full dislocation at vein 2 and green tornal scales, and spots 5, 6 and 7 are broadly in line (not widely out of line). The only distinguishing character is spot 6 position: equidistant/midway (epimuta) vs still clearly nearer to spot 5 (metamuta).

choices.label: Equidistant — spot 6 roughly midway between spot 5 and the end-cell bar
Not displaced / Slightly displaced — spot 6 roughly level with spot 5, or only minimally shifted inward; spots 5, 6 and 7 broadly in line

q_tailless_partial_muta_check.question: Are the hindwing underside postdiscal spots more quadrate/banded in shape, rather than rounded?
q_tailless_partial_muta_check.hint: In the muta subgroup ([A. moorei busa](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=780493&preferred_place_id=6734), [A. muta maranda](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=780491&preferred_place_id=6734), [A. tropaea](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=780493&preferred_place_id=6734)), the HW postdiscal spots are noticeably rectangular or banded rather than round. In the agelastus and agesilaus groups, the spots are more rounded or oval. Check the HW underside only — FW visibility is not required.

choices.label: Yes — HW spots more quadrate or banded in shape (muta subgroup)
No — HW spots more rounded in shape (agelastus or agesilaus group)

q_tailless_agelastus_check.question: Is the forewing underside postdiscal band continuous and unbroken, often with a small spot at the base of space 3?
q_tailless_agelastus_check.hint: The agelastus group has a continuous (non-dislocated) FW postdiscal band, often with a small spot at the base of FW space 3; most species lack a costal spot in FW space 10 (exception: epimete suedas). The agesilaus group ([A. agesilaus gesa](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1278725&preferred_place_id=6734), [A. avatha](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1062607&preferred_place_id=6734)) has a dislocated FW postdiscal band — spots shift at a vein, breaking the smooth row.

choices.label: Yes — FW postdiscal band continuous (agelastus group)
No — FW postdiscal band dislocated or broken (agesilaus group)

q_tailless_not_slightly_check.question: Is the HW postdiscal band at vein 2 fully separated into two distinct portions — a clear gap visible between them?
q_tailless_not_slightly_check.hint: In [A. metamuta metamuta](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1340245&preferred_place_id=6734), even though spot 6 is only slightly displaced, the HW postdiscal band at vein 2 is fully broken into two distinct portions with a clear gap. In the camdeo, amphimuta and most other non-displaced tailless groups, the band is continuous across vein 2 with no such gap.

choices.label: Yes — HW postdiscal band fully separated at vein 2 (two distinct portions with a clear gap; metamuta type)
No — HW postdiscal band continuous or not clearly separated at vein 2

q_tailless_camdeo.question: Is there a restricted dark patch in FW space 1b (under the forewing cell), bounded outwardly by a pale line?
q_tailless_camdeo.hint: A small number of camdeo group species are tailless ([A. belphoebe cowani](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+belphoebe), [A. myrzalina](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1564116&preferred_place_id=6734)). The camdeo group is identified by a restricted dark patch in FW underside space 1b with a clearly defined pale bordering line. In field photos this area is often hidden under the hindwing — if FW space 1b is not clearly visible, select Cannot determine; the key will continue without this character.

choices.label: Yes — restricted dark patch present in FW space 1b with pale bordering line (camdeo group)
No — no dark patch present in FW space 1b
Cannot determine — FW space 1b obscured by hindwing

q_tailless_echelon.question: Do hindwing postdiscal spots 5, 6, and 7 overlap with their centres forming a straight line (echelon arrangement)?

question_link.text: forming a straight line

q_tailless_echelon.hint: The amphimuta group (muta, perimuta, kurzi, and amphimuta subgroups) is defined by HW spots 5, 6, and 7 having their centres in a straight line — an 'echelon'. Most amphimuta-group species are tailless. If present, this character routes to a subgroup choice.

choices.label: Yes — HW spots 5, 6, and 7 in echelon (centres in straight line)
No — spots not in echelon

q_tailless_rama.question: Is hindwing postdiscal spot 6 outwardly sinuous or concave, and is there a costal spot present in forewing underside space 10, above the end-cell bar?

question_link.text: forewing underside space 10

q_tailless_rama.hint: [Arhopala arvina adalitas](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1294761&preferred_place_id=6734) is the only tailless species in the rama group. Its key underside characters are an S-shaped (outwardly sinuous or concave) HW postdiscal spot 6 and a costal spot in FW space 10, above the end-cell bar. Underside ground colour is hair-brown; tornal green scales are present. FW ~18.0 mm — slightly larger than [A. antimuta antimuta](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=498184&preferred_place_id=6734) (FW ~16.0 mm), which also has spot 6 widely out of line but instead has the HW postdiscal band completely dislocated at vein 2 (vs only partially dislocated in [A. arvina adalitas](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1294761&preferred_place_id=6734)).

choices.label: Yes — HW spot 6 outwardly sinuous or concave; costal spot in FW space 10 above the end-cell bar
No
Cannot determine

g_centaurus.description: Separated from all other groups by having the FW cell spots outlined in greenish silver on the underside. HW cell short — significantly less than half the wing length. One Peninsular Malaysian species: [A. centaurus](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=146903&preferred_place_id=6734), represented by two subspecies (nakula on the mainland; dixoni restricted to Tioman Island).
key_features: FW cell spots outlined in greenish silver (underside)
HW cell short: noticeably less than half wing length
Tailed

g_anthelus.description: Forewing postdiscal band only partially dislocated at vein 4. Spot present at base of FW space 10; NO spot at base of HW space 6. Two Malayan species: [A. anthelus grahami](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=149211&preferred_place_id=6734) (large, 28 mm; extra spots in FW space 11 and HW space 8) and [A. achelous achelous](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=940963&preferred_place_id=6734) (smaller, 22 mm; no extra spots).
key_features: FW postdiscal band partially dislocated at vein 4
Spot at base of FW space 10
No spot at base of HW space 6

g_camdeo_general.description: Defined by a dark area under the forewing cell bounded outwardly by a pale line across space 1b, with HW postdiscal spots 7, 6, and 5 arranged in echelon. No myrzala or bazaloides subgroup character is visible. Most species here belong to the camdeo subgroup (9 species, the largest subgroup), which often features a small spot at the extreme base of FW space 3 — though this character is usually hidden under the hindwing. Continue below to key out the individual species.
key_features: Dark area under FW cell (space 1b) bounded by pale line
HW postdiscal spots 7, 6, 5 in echelon
Small spot sometimes at extreme base of FW space 3 (camdeo subgroup; often obscured)

g_camdeo_camdeo.description: The largest camdeo subgroup (9 species). Often features a small spot at the extreme base of FW space 3. Includes both tailed and tailless species and a wide range of upperside colours from pale silvery-blue to deep bluish-purple.
key_features: Dark area under FW cell (space 1b) bounded by pale line
Small spot often at extreme base of FW space 3
HW postdiscal spots 7, 6, 5 in echelon

g_camdeo_myrzala.description: Easily recognized by the crimson-brown underside with markings outlined by prominent white striae, and a spot always present at the base of HW space 6. Two species separated by tail presence and border width.
key_features: Crimson-brown underside with markings outlined by white striae
Spot at base of HW space 6
Dark area under FW cell (space 1b)

g_camdeo_bazaloides.description: Unique within the camdeo group for having an extra spot in mid-space 8 on the HW underside. Underside is purple-brown with white striae but LACKS the HW space 6 basal spot that defines the myrzala subgroup. One species.
key_features: Extra spot mid-space 8 on HW underside
Purple-brown underside with white striae
No spot at base of HW space 6 (unlike myrzala subgroup)
Dark area under FW cell (space 1b)

g_agesias.description: Tailless, with slightly dentate (toothed) HW margins. Both sexes similar: shining, powdery blue with broad black borders (~1.5 mm at tornus, expanding to mid-costa). Prominent rounded spots on the underside; at least one subcostal spot in FW space 11. FW vein 9 notably short. Three Malaysian species.
key_features: Tailless (HW margins slightly dentate)
Sexes alike: shining powdery blue; broad black borders
At least one subcostal spot above FW cell in space 11
FW vein 9 notably short

g_ijanensis.description: Tailed species with an unusually long hindwing cell (~half wing length or longer). Underside: spot in subcostal mid-space 11 above the central cell spot; no spot at base of FW space 10; spot always at base of HW space 6. The male FW border runs along the costa to the wing base.
key_features: Tailed
HW cell ~half wing length or longer
FW underside: subcostal spot in space 11; no spot at base of space 10
Spot at base of HW space 6
Male: FW costal border runs to wing base

g_epimuta.description: Males are instantly recognizable by a discal patch on the forewing upperside that is differently coloured and more opaque than the surrounding blue (dense fuscous scales underlying the blue). On the underside, HW postdiscal spot 6 is placed midway between spot 5 and the end-cell bar. Two species are tailed; one is tailless.
key_features: Male: opaque or differently-coloured discal patch on FW upperside
HW postdiscal spot 6 midway between spot 5 and end-cell bar
Two tailed spp. (atosia, lurida); one tailless sp. (epimuta)

g_agesilaus.description: Tailless species that can be confused with the amphimuta group. Defined by HW postdiscal spot 6 placed equidistantly between spot 5 and the end-cell bar. HW postdiscal band only partially dislocated at vein 2. Two Malaysian species.
key_features: Tailless
HW postdiscal spot 6 equidistant between spot 5 and end-cell bar
HW postdiscal band only partially dislocated at vein 2

q_agesilaus_s1.question: Is a basal cell spot present on the forewing underside?
q_agesilaus_s1.hint: [Arhopala agesilaus gesa](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1278725&preferred_place_id=6734) has a basal cell spot on the FW underside (typical for the genus). [Arhopala avatha](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1062607&preferred_place_id=6734) uniquely lacks this basal cell spot — its absence is diagnostic.

choices.label: Yes — basal cell spot present on FW underside
No — basal cell spot absent (unique within this group)

r_agesilaus_gesa.name: Arhopala agesilaus gesa
r_agesilaus_gesa.note: Tailless. FW underside has a basal cell spot. Male: rich violet blue; FW black border 2.0 mm at tornus expanding to 4.0 mm at apex. Female: bluer shade than male. Superficially resembles a small [Arhopala major](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=429756&preferred_place_id=6734).

features.On the hindwing underside, what best des: Spot 6 touching or overlapping the end-cell bar; or spots 5, 6 and 7 widely out of line / displaced

r_avatha.name: Arhopala avatha
r_avatha.note: Tailless. Uniquely lacks the basal cell spot on FW underside. Male: very dark bluish violet; FW black border 1.0 mm at tornus increasing to 2.0 mm at apex (narrow borders). Female: purple. Similar to [Arhopala antimuta](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=498184&preferred_place_id=6734) but distinguished by wider borders in the male.

features.On the hindwing underside, what best des: Spot 6 touching or overlapping the end-cell bar; or spots 5, 6 and 7 widely out of line / displaced

g_oenea.description: FW apex acute and termen straight between veins 2–4. Underside postdiscal spots narrow, regular, and rectangular; FW band sinuous or only slightly dislocated at vein 4. Monotypic in the Peninsula. ⚠ Note: the Malayan representative (allata pandora) is tailed — routing via the tailless chain may need review.
key_features: FW apex acute; termen straight between veins 2–4
Underside postdiscal spots narrow, regular, rectangular
FW band sinuous or slightly dislocated at vein 4
Tailed (allata pandora)

g_amphimuta_muta.description: Postdiscal spots are more quadrate (squarish/banded). The FW postdiscal spot in space 2 is stepped outward toward the termen, offset from the main band. Three Malaysian species.
key_features: HW spots 5, 6, 7 in echelon (centres in straight line)
Spots more quadrate/banded in shape
FW space 2 spot stepped outward toward termen

g_amphimuta_perimuta.description: Tailless. FW postdiscal spots in spaces 2 and 3 are in line with each other (may be oblique); spot 4 shifted distad. Three Malaysian species, one ([A. perimuta regina](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=448212&preferred_place_id=6734)) immediately recognized by a yellowish discal area on the HW underside.
key_features: Tailless
HW spots 5, 6, 7 in echelon (centres overlapping, in a straight line)
FW postdiscal spots 2 and 3 in line; spot 4 shifted distad
[A. perimuta](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=448212&preferred_place_id=6734): yellowish HW discal area; markings obscure; no tornal green scales

g_hypomuta.description: Tailless species, often confused with the amphimuta group. Most Malayan individuals are distinguished by a small spot at the extreme base of HW space 6 on the underside (this spot is usually absent in Sumatran and Bornean specimens). Monotypic in the Malay Peninsula.
key_features: Tailless
Usually: small spot at extreme base of HW space 6 (Malayan ssp.)
FW ~19 mm

g_inornata.description: Tailless with a very rounded hindwing tornus. On the underside: no tornal green scales; HW postdiscal spot 6 placed immediately below spot 7. Monotypic.
key_features: Tailless; very rounded HW tornus
No tornal green scales on underside
HW spot 6 placed immediately below spot 7
FW ~21 mm

g_democritus.description: Underside markings macular — separated lines and dots. Short HW tail 1.5–2.0 mm. Costal spot in mid-FW space 11; FW spot 4 shifted distad; HW spot 6 outwardly concave; HW band widely dislocated at vein 2. Two Malayan subspecies of [A. democritus](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=622433&preferred_place_id=6734) separated by underside colour.
key_features: Macular underside (separated dots and lines)
Short HW tail 1.5–2.0 mm
Costal spot in mid-FW space 11; FW spot 4 shifted distad
HW spot 6 outwardly concave; HW band widely dislocated at vein 2

g_alitaeus.description: Defined by having a central spot in FW space 11 on the underside and HW postdiscal spot 6 outwardly straight or convex. Tailed species (tail 3.5–4.0 mm, except [A. denta](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1192142&preferred_place_id=6734) ~2.5 mm). Nine Malaysian species.
key_features: Central spot in FW underside space 11
HW postdiscal spot 6 outwardly straight or convex
Tailed (3.5–4.0 mm; [A. denta](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1192142&preferred_place_id=6734) only ~2.5 mm)
Male aedeagus right-angled before apex (dissection required)

g_agrata_ace.description: Recognizable by the hindwing underside central cell spot, which is band-like and stretches entirely across the cell. Two Malaysian species, separated by the FW postdiscal band dislocation and the shape of HW postdiscal spot 6.
key_features: HW underside central cell spot band-like, stretching entirely across the cell

q_agrata_ace_s1.question: Is the forewing postdiscal band clearly dislocated at vein 4, and is hindwing postdiscal spot 6 outwardly concave?

question_link.text: forewing postdiscal band clearly dislocated at vein 4

q_agrata_ace_s1.hint: [Arhopala ace ace](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=785837&preferred_place_id=6734) has the FW band distinctly dislocated at vein 4 with HW spot 6 outwardly concave; all markings are relatively wide and the male is deep cobalt blue with a thread-like border. [Arhopala azinis azinis](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+azinis) has the FW band sinuous or only slightly dislocated at vein 4, HW spot 6 outwardly straight or convex, and the male has broad borders; it is strictly montane.

choices.label: Yes — FW band clearly dislocated at vein 4; HW spot 6 outwardly concave
No — FW band sinuous or only slightly dislocated; HW spot 6 straight or convex

r_ace_ace.name: Arhopala ace ace
r_ace_ace.note: Underside: all markings relatively wide; FW postdiscal band dislocated at vein 4; HW postdiscal spot 6 outwardly concave. Male: deep cobalt blue with black border reduced to a thread. Female: purple.

r_azinis_azinis.name: Arhopala azinis azinis
r_azinis_azinis.note: Underside: FW postdiscal band sinuous or only slightly dislocated at vein 4; HW postdiscal spot 6 outwardly straight or convex. Male: dark blue with broad black borders (2.0 mm at FW mid-termen, 3.0 mm on HW). Female: purple. Strictly montane habitat.

g_agrata_agrata.description: Identified by specific absences on the forewing underside: the postdiscal spot in space 9 is absent or very obscure, and there is never a costal spot in space 10. One Malaysian species.
key_features: FW underside: postdiscal spot in space 9 absent or very obscure
FW underside: no costal spot in space 10
HW postdiscal spot 6 considerably wider than spot 7
All underside markings tend to be rather faint

r_agrata_agrata.name: Arhopala agrata agrata
r_agrata_agrata.note: Underside markings faint. FW: postdiscal spot in space 9 absent or very obscure; no costal spot in space 10. HW: postdiscal spot 6 considerably wider than spot 7. Male: dark purplish blue with black border reduced to a thread. Female: bright blue to purple-blue; HW blue reaches the termen between vein endings.

features.On the hindwing underside, is the postdi: No — spot 6 displaced past midway; touching or overlapping the end-cell bar; spots 5, 6 and 7 widely out of line

g_cleander.description: Large species (FW > 21 mm) with a conspicuously projecting HW tornal lobe. HW postdiscal band completely dislocated at vein 2. Four species separated by FW band dislocation at vein 4 and by HW spot 6 position relative to spots 5 and the end-cell bar.
key_features: FW > 21 mm; conspicuously projecting HW tornal lobe
HW postdiscal band completely dislocated at vein 2
HW spots 6 and 7 placed one below the other

g_cleander_alea.description: Smaller species (FW < 21 mm) within the cleander group with a less conspicuous HW tornal lobe. Nine species with diverse underside colours from slatey purple to slatey pink; genitalia often needed for certain identification.
key_features: FW < 21 mm; HW tornal lobe less conspicuous
HW postdiscal band completely dislocated at vein 2
Male genitalia often required for certain ID

q_tailed_spot4_outofline.question: Are the underside postdiscal spots uniformly narrow and rectangular throughout the band, combined with an acute FW apex and nearly straight termen (between veins 2–4)?
q_tailed_spot4_outofline.hint: This question separates the oenea group ([A. allata pandora](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=847055&preferred_place_id=6734) — monotypic on the Peninsula) from the eumolphus group. The oenea group has a COMBINATION of (1) underside postdiscal spots that are narrow, elongate, and rectangular throughout the band, and (2) an acute FW apex with termen nearly straight between veins 2 and 4. Important: the eumolphus group contains BOTH rounded-apex species (e.g. [A. eumolphus](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=498188&preferred_place_id=6734), [A. acta](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+acta)) AND acute-apex species (e.g. [A. tameanga](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+tameanga), [A. hellenore](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=883689&preferred_place_id=6734)) — so apex shape alone is not sufficient. If the underside spots are not uniformly narrow and rectangular, answer 'No' regardless of apex shape.

choices.label: Yes — underside spots uniformly narrow and rectangular; FW apex acute, termen nearly straight
No — underside spots not uniformly narrow rectangular (eumolphus group, rounded or acute apex)

g_eumolphus.description: Tailed species where the forewing postdiscal spot 4 is moved out of line with the others. On the hindwing, postdiscal spot 6 is placed directly below spot 7. This group includes species with shining green males.
key_features: Tailed
FW postdiscal spot 4 out of line with the other spots
HW postdiscal spot 6 placed directly below spot 7
Some males: shining green upperside

g_vihara.description: Tailed. FW 20–22 mm. Distinguished from the similar eumolphus group by FW postdiscal spot 4 being in line with spots 5 and 6 (not moved out of line). Also lacks a costal spot in FW space 10. HW postdiscal spot 6 rounded, more or less separated. HW band only partially dislocated at vein 2. Monotypic.
key_features: Tailed; FW 20–22 mm
FW spot 4 in line with spots 5–6 (cf. eumolphus: spot 4 out of line)
No costal spot in FW space 10
HW spot 6 rounded, more or less separated
HW band partially dislocated at vein 2

g_rama.description: HW postdiscal spot 6 is outwardly sinuous or concave. A costal spot is normally present in FW space 10 on the underside. Three rare Malayan species: one tailless (arvina), two tailed (paralea 2.5 mm, buddha 1.0 mm).
key_features: HW postdiscal spot 6 outwardly sinuous or concave
Costal spot normally present in FW space 10
Three spp.: one tailless, two tailed (different tail lengths)

g_agelastus.description: FW underside postdiscal band is continuous; often a spot at base of FW space 3. Most species lack a costal spot in FW space 10 (exception: epimete suedas). HW postdiscal band only partially dislocated at vein 2. Most species are tailless; males typically have broad black borders. Eight Malaysian species in four subgroups.
key_features: FW postdiscal band continuous
HW postdiscal band partially dislocated at vein 2
Most species tailless (barami penanga and alesia sacharja are the rare tailed exceptions, both keyed in the tailed branch)
Males: broad black borders, often similar to females

g_corinda.description: Monotypic. Filamentous HW tail ~3.5 mm. FW: pointed apex, straight termen. FW underside: basal half of space 1b completely darkened (large purple-brown patch); spot 4 shifted distad; no costal spot in FW space 10. HW underside: spot 6 outwardly convex; band partially dislocated at vein 2. Male deep violet/dark purple, very narrow border.
key_features: Filamentous HW tail ~3.5 mm
FW underside: large purple-brown patch in basal half of space 1b
FW spot 4 shifted distad; no costal spot in space 10
HW spot 6 outwardly convex; band partially dislocated at vein 2

g_aurea.description: Males have a HW cell considerably longer than half the wing length, a rather acute FW apex, and a straight or slightly concave termen. Males are shining green above; females are blue or purple-blue. All members possess a short stumpy HW tail (just under 2.0 mm). Five species (4 Peninsular, 1 Bornean).
key_features: Male: HW cell considerably longer than half wing length
Male: FW apex acute; termen straight or slightly concave
Male upperside: shining green; female blue or purple-blue
Short stumpy HW tail (~2 mm)

g_ganesa.description: Small tailed species. Underside FW has a characteristic spot at the base of space 11, positioned between the basal and central cell spots, plus a spot at the base of space 3. Keyed within the group first by whether the gap between the outer and central spots in HW space 7 is conspicuously whitened — present in [A. ammon](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=506221&preferred_place_id=6734), [A. ammonides](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=470744&preferred_place_id=6734), and [A. elizabethae](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+elizabethae); absent in [A. ariel](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=885911&preferred_place_id=6734) and [A. paraganesa](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=333568&preferred_place_id=6734).
key_features: Small; short HW tail
FW underside: spot at base of space 11 (between basal and central cell spots)
FW underside: spot at base of space 3

g_fulla.description: Tailless species instantly recognisable by the near-total suppression of underside markings. The usual postdiscal spots on both wings are replaced by a faint, obscure streak. The rectangular mark at the end of the forewing discal cell is absent (and may also be absent on the hindwing). Only a few obscure basal spots remain near the base of the hindwing. Monotypic in Malaysia.
key_features: Tailless
Underside markings almost entirely absent
Postdiscal spots replaced by a faint obscure streak on both wings
No rectangular mark at the end of the forewing discal cell
Only a few faint basal spots remain near the HW base
Only one Malaysian species: [Arhopala fulla intaca](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=880792&preferred_place_id=6734)

g_abseus.description: Features a short white-tipped tail at vein 3 of the hindwing (not the usual vein 2), and a spot at the base of hindwing space 1a. Forewing vein 9 is very short, arising well beyond the end of vein 10. Two species in Malaysia.
key_features: Short white-tipped tail at HW vein 3
Spot at base of HW space 1a
FW vein 9 very short (arises well beyond end of vein 10)

q_abseus_species.question: What is the underside ground colour and are the markings darkened?
q_abseus_species.hint: Two species share the vein 3 white-tipped tail. abseus has strongly darkened (blackish-brown) underside markings and a costal spot in FW space 12. anella is larger with hair-brown underside and no spot in FW space 12.

choices.label: Markings much darkened (blackish brown); costal spot present in FW space 12
Underside hair brown; spots large and rounded; no spot in FW space 12; male with very wide fuscous borders on upperside

r_abseus_abseus.name: Arhopala abseus
r_abseus_abseus.note: Underside markings much darkened — most spots blackish brown. Forewing underside: costal spot in space 12; two spots each in spaces 9, 10, and 11. Hindwing: spot in middle of space 7 outlined in whitish. Male upperside: deep lustrous violet blue; FW black border expands from 2.0 mm at tornus to 4.0 mm at apex. Female: shining blue with wider borders.

r_anella.name: Arhopala anella
r_anella.note: Considerably larger than [A. abseus](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=333427&preferred_place_id=6734). Underside ground colour hair brown; markings not prominently darkened; no spot in FW space 12. Male upperside: blue restricted to basal halves of FW spaces 1b, 2, and 3 and the lower half of the cell; HW blue confined almost entirely to the cell; very wide fuscous borders. Female: relatively narrow black borders.

g_aedias.description: Characterised by a very long filamentous tail at vein 2 (~6.0 mm). HW postdiscal spots 5, 6, and 7 are in echelon. No dark area under the FW cell, and no spot at the base of HW space 6. One Malaysian species.
key_features: Long filamentous tail at vein 2 (~6.0 mm)
HW postdiscal spots 5, 6, and 7 in echelon
No dark area under the FW cell
No spot at base of HW space 6

r_aedias.name: Arhopala aedias
r_aedias.note: Underside ground colour hair brown; spots narrowly outlined in pale buff lines. Male upperside: lustrous violet blue with black border reduced to a thread. Female upperside: purple blue with typical broad black borders. Two size forms occur: large (FW 25–29 mm) and small (FW 20–22 mm; small form has more rounded, darker spots beneath). Two subspecies: [A. aedias agnis](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=498179&preferred_place_id=6734) (forest at low to moderate elevations, standard) and [A. aedias meritatas](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=498179&preferred_place_id=6734) (Langkawi Islands — paler and brighter blue upperside in both sexes).

features.On the hindwing underside, is postdiscal: No — HW spots 5, 6 and 7 in echelon; spot 6 nearer to spot 5
features.What is the underside ground colour and: Hair brown; spots narrowly buff-outlined; large (FW 20–29 mm); rounded FW apex; tail ~6 mm — A. aedias
features.Is there a restricted dark patch in FW s: No — no dark patch present in FW space 1b
features.What does the hindwing underside look li: Brown or grey-brown underside; HW spot 6 clearly nearer to spot 5 than to the end-cell bar — no crimson colouring and no extra spot in space 8

q_agelastus_s1.question: Is there a costal spot in forewing space 10?

question_link.text: costal spot in forewing space 10

q_agelastus_s1.hint: A costal spot in FW space 10 is unique to [Arhopala epimete suedas](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+epimete) within this group. All other agelastus group species lack it.

choices.label: Yes — costal spot present in FW space 10
No — FW space 10 lacks a costal spot

q_agelastus_s3.question: Are the underside markings small and macular (dot-like), and are tornal green scales absent from the hindwing?
q_agelastus_s3.hint: [Arhopala cardoni](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+cardoni) has characteristically small, macular (isolated dot-like) markings on the underside. The hindwing also lacks the metallic tornal green scales seen in some other species. The HW is evenly rounded.

choices.label: Yes — small macular markings; no tornal green scales; HW evenly rounded
No — markings larger or not macular

q_agelastus_hw45.question: On the hindwing underside, are the inner submarginal spots in spaces 4–5 notably enlarged and darkened?

question_link.text: inner submarginal spots

q_agelastus_hw45.hint: This is usually the most field-visible diagnostic character for [Arhopala alaconia media](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=880741&preferred_place_id=6734) (alesia subgroup) — the matching FW spaces 2–3 spots are often hidden under the hindwing in field photos, so the HW spaces 4–5 spots alone can usually nail down the species. If the HW spaces 4–5 spots are clearly enlarged and darkened, this is decisive. If unclear or not visible, the next question offers FW spots and HW spot 6 shape as backup characters.

choices.label: Yes — HW spaces 4–5 submarginal spots notably enlarged and darkened
No / cannot determine

q_agelastus_s4.question: Are the inner submarginal spots in FW spaces 2–3 notably enlarged and darkened, and is HW postdiscal spot 6 outwardly concave or sinuous?

question_link.text: inner submarginal spots

q_agelastus_s4.hint: [Arhopala alaconia media](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=880741&preferred_place_id=6734) (alesia subgroup) is distinguished by strikingly enlarged and darkened inner submarginal spots in these specific spaces, combined with an outwardly concave or sinuous HW postdiscal spot 6. Note: the only other Malayan member of this subgroup, [A. alesia sacharja](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+alesia), is a tailed exception keyed earlier — in the tailed branch, alongside [A. barami penanga](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=958932&preferred_place_id=6734).

choices.label: Yes — FW inner submarginal spots enlarged and darkened; HW spot 6 outwardly concave or sinuous
No

q_agelastus_s5.question: Is the male upperside notably darker violet with narrower black borders than typical for the group?
q_agelastus_s5.hint: [A. labuana](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+labuana) is a Bornean endemic with a notably darker violet male and narrower black borders than other members of this group. If the male upperside is not visible, choose 'Cannot determine' to continue — but note that if the specimen is from Borneo, [A. labuana](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+labuana) should be considered.

choices.label: Yes — much darker violet; borders notably narrower
No — violet blue
Cannot determine — upperside not visible

q_agelastus_s6.question: How broad is the male forewing black border?
q_agelastus_s6.hint: Wildeyana has broader borders (2.0 mm at tornus to 5.0 mm at costa) than agelastus (1.0 mm at tornus to 4.0 mm at apex). Females: wildeyana is brighter/purer blue; agelastus female is brighter blue with broader borders; in agelastus, the underside HW space 3 spot is nearly in line with spots 2 and 4.

choices.label: Broad: 2.0 mm at tornus to 5.0 mm at costa
Standard: 1.0 mm at tornus to 4.0 mm at apex
Cannot determine — upperside not visible

r_epimete_suedas.name: Arhopala epimete suedas
r_epimete_suedas.note: Unique in the agelastus group: costal spot present in FW space 10. FW underside: lower portion of postdiscal band significantly wider than upper part. HW lacks tornal green scales. Both sexes alike: blue with broad FW borders (2.0 mm at tornus to 6.0 mm at costa). Blue on HW confined to the cell. Tailless.

features.On the hindwing underside, what best des: Spot 6 touching or overlapping the end-cell bar; or spots 5, 6 and 7 widely out of line / displaced

r_barami_penanga.name: Arhopala barami penanga
r_barami_penanga.note: One of two tailed exceptions in the otherwise-tailless agelastus group; relatively large for the group (FW 22.0–23.0 mm). Underside: HW postdiscal spot 6 widely out of line with spots 5 and 7 — inner edge of spot 6 in line with, or even further inward (basad) than, the inner edge of spot 7; spot 6 outwardly convex and typically touches or overlaps the end-cell bar. FW postdiscal band continuous; no costal spot in space 10; small spot often present at base of space 3; inner submarginal spots uniform in size and colour. [A. alesia sacharja](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+alesia) shares this exact spot-6/submarginal-spot pattern (per field-photo review, correcting older literature) — separate by size and border width: A. barami penanga is larger (FW 22.0–23.0 mm) with an exceptionally broad male border expanding from 2.5 mm at the tornus to 8.0 mm at the costa, vs. A. alesia sacharja's smaller size and more moderate border. Female: paler blue with similarly broad borders.

features.On the hindwing underside, is the postdi: No — spot 6 displaced past midway; touching or overlapping the end-cell bar; spots 5, 6 and 7 widely out of line

r_cardoni.name: Arhopala cardoni
r_cardoni.note: Tailless; evenly rounded HW tornus with no tooth or tail at vein 2. Underside markings small and macular (dot-like); no tornal green scales. Male: bright blue with violet tinge; FW border 2.0 mm at tornus expanding to 5.0 mm at apex. Female: purer blue with slightly wider black borders than the male.

features.On the hindwing underside, what best des: Spot 6 touching or overlapping the end-cell bar; or spots 5, 6 and 7 widely out of line / displaced

r_alaconia_media.name: Arhopala alaconia media
