r_alaconia_media.note: Alesia subgroup (agelastus group) — the long-established Malayan member of this subgroup, and (per field-photo review) the only Malayan agelastus-group species that genuinely has a concave/sinuous HW spot 6 paired with enlarged submarginal spots; [A. alesia sacharja](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+alesia), despite older literature placing it in the same subgroup, actually has a convex spot 6 touching the end-cell bar and uniform (not enlarged) submarginal spots — see notebook correction. Distinctive early-exit character (per field-photo review): hindwing underside spaces 4–5 submarginal spots prominently black and wedge-shaped (∧), with the postdiscal spots large and edged by a thick, pale border — unique to this species among Malaysian Arhopala. Underside: inner submarginal spots in FW spaces 2–3 and HW spaces 4–5 notably enlarged and darkened; HW postdiscal spot 6 outwardly concave or sinuous; tornal green scales present on the hindwing; small spot often at base of FW space 3. This is the normal tailless form (slightly toothed); see Arhopala alaconia media f. kempi for the rarer tailed form (~2.5 mm tail at vein 2), which is reached directly in the tailed branch via this same wedge-shaped submarginal-spot character. Both sexes alike: violet blue with FW border 3.0 mm at tornus expanding to mid-costa.

features.On the hindwing underside, what best des: Spot 6 touching or overlapping the end-cell bar; or spots 5, 6 and 7 widely out of line / displaced

r_alaconia_kempi.name: Arhopala alaconia media f. kempi
r_alaconia_kempi.note: Alesia subgroup (agelastus group) — the rarer tailed form (f. kempi, ~2.5 mm tail at vein 2) of Arhopala alaconia media; though described as rare in the literature, it is reportedly encountered fairly often in the field. Same diagnostic characters as the tailless form: hindwing underside spaces 4–5 submarginal spots prominently black and wedge-shaped (∧), with the postdiscal spots large and edged by a thick, pale border — unique to this species among Malaysian Arhopala; inner submarginal spots in FW spaces 2–3 and HW spaces 4–5 notably enlarged and darkened; HW postdiscal spot 6 outwardly concave or sinuous; tornal green scales present on the hindwing; small spot often at base of FW space 3. Both sexes alike: violet blue with FW border 3.0 mm at tornus expanding to mid-costa.

r_alesia_sacharja.name: Arhopala alesia sacharja
r_alesia_sacharja.note: Agelastus group, alongside [A. barami penanga](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=958932&preferred_place_id=6734) one of only two tailed exceptions in the otherwise-tailless group. Tail at the usual vein 2 position (not the unusual short white-tipped vein-3 tail of the abseus group). A very rare, late Malayan record (first found in Langkawi in 1994; also distributed across NE India, Myanmar, Thailand, Laos, and Vietnam). Underside (per field-photo review, correcting older literature that described a concave spot 6 and enlarged submarginal spots — see notebook correction): ground colour pale grey-brown, distinctly paler than the typical hair-brown Arhopala underside — a useful early-exit character, since its only close rival A. barami penanga has a typical hair-brown underside with solid spots darker than the ground colour; FW postdiscal band almost entirely dark/black, strongly contrasting against the pale ground colour, continuous, often with a small spot at the base of FW space 3; HW postdiscal band only slightly dislocated at vein 2 (shifted but still touching); inner submarginal spots in HW spaces 4–5 and the end-cell bar very pale, close to the ground colour with little contrast, each ringed by a black border and then a white outer ring; HW postdiscal spot 6 outwardly convex, touching or overlapping the end-cell bar, spots 5/6/7 widely out of line — effectively identical to A. barami penanga here. Separated from A. barami penanga by being smaller, with a more moderate male FW border (~3.0 mm at tornus, not reaching barami's extreme 8.0 mm at costa). Sexes alike: violet blue.

features.On the hindwing underside, is the postdi: No — spot 6 displaced past midway; touching or overlapping the end-cell bar; spots 5, 6 and 7 widely out of line

r_labuana.name: Arhopala labuana
r_labuana.note: Male upperside: much darker violet than [A. agelastus](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1294319&preferred_place_id=6734), with narrower black borders. Female: dark blue. Tailless. Underside: HW postdiscal spot in space 3 is usually shifted well basad (inward), placing it significantly out of line with spots 2 and 4 — the primary diagnostic separating it from [A. agelastus](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1294319&preferred_place_id=6734), whose spot 3 sits almost in line with spots 2 and 4.

features.On the hindwing underside, what best des: Spot 6 touching or overlapping the end-cell bar; or spots 5, 6 and 7 widely out of line / displaced
features.Is the hindwing underside postdiscal spo: No — spot 3 displaced, not clearly in line

r_wildeyana.name: Arhopala wildeyana wildeyana
r_wildeyana.note: Tailless. Male upperside: violet blue with broader borders than [A. labuana](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+labuana) or [A. agelastus](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1294319&preferred_place_id=6734) — 2.0 mm at tornus to 5.0 mm at costa. Female: brighter and purer blue. Underside: HW postdiscal spots 5, 6 and 7 widely out of line; the inner edge of spot 6 is in line with, or even further inward (basad) than, the inner edge of spot 7; spot 6 is outwardly convex and typically touches or overlaps the end-cell bar. Agelastus group; HW postdiscal band only partially dislocated at vein 2; FW postdiscal band continuous.

features.On the hindwing underside, what best des: Spot 6 touching or overlapping the end-cell bar; or spots 5, 6 and 7 widely out of line / displaced
features.Is the HW postdiscal band only partially: Yes — band only partially dislocated at vein 2 (shifted but connected)
features.Is the forewing underside postdiscal ban: Yes — FW postdiscal band continuous (agelastus group)

r_agelastus_agelastus.name: Arhopala agelastus agelastus
r_agelastus_agelastus.note: Underside: HW postdiscal spot in space 3 is almost in line with spots in spaces 2 and 4 (distinguishes female from allied species). Male: violet blue with FW border 1.0 mm at tornus to 4.0 mm at apex. Female: brighter blue with broader borders. Tailless.

features.On the hindwing underside, what best des: Spot 6 touching or overlapping the end-cell bar; or spots 5, 6 and 7 widely out of line / displaced
features.Is the hindwing underside postdiscal spo: Yes — spot 3 almost in line with spots 2 and 4

q_agesias_s1.question: How many spots are in forewing space 11, and what is the underside ground colour?

question_link.text: forewing space 11

q_agesias_s1.hint: [Arhopala kinabala](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=881533&preferred_place_id=6734) is distinguished by having TWO spots in FW space 11, a dark brown ground colour, and blackish-brown markings. [A. agesias](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=149210&preferred_place_id=6734) and [A. similis](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=149215&preferred_place_id=6734) have a single spot in space 11 and a hair-brown ground colour.

choices.label: Two spots in FW space 11; ground colour dark brown; markings blackish-brown
Single spot in FW space 11; ground colour hair brown

q_agesias_s2.question: Is the postdiscal band completely absent from the forewing underside?
q_agesias_s2.hint: [Arhopala similis](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=149215&preferred_place_id=6734) is superficially identical to [A. agesias](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=149210&preferred_place_id=6734) but uniquely lacks the postdiscal band entirely on the FW underside. [A. agesias](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=149210&preferred_place_id=6734) has the normal rounded-spot postdiscal band present.

choices.label: Yes — postdiscal band completely absent from FW underside
No — postdiscal band present (rounded spots)

r_kinabala.name: Arhopala kinabala
r_kinabala.note: Tailless. Underside ground colour dark brown with large, blackish-brown markings. FW space 11 has two spots; FW space 3 spot is elongate and touches the end-cell bar. HW postdiscal spot 6 is oval, significantly enlarged, and overlaps the end-cell bar. Rare; seldom found below 450 m elevation.

r_similis.name: Arhopala similis
r_similis.note: Tailless. Superficially identical to [A. agesias](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=149210&preferred_place_id=6734) but uniquely lacks the postdiscal band on the FW underside. Status as a separate species (vs. dimorph of [A. agesias](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=149210&preferred_place_id=6734)) has been questioned, though female genitalia differ slightly.

features.On the hindwing underside, what best des: Equidistant — spot 6 roughly midway between spot 5 and the end-cell bar

r_agesias.name: Arhopala agesias
r_agesias.note: Tailless. The most common species in the group. Underside: hair-brown ground colour with all markings as rounded spots; single central spot in FW space 11.

features.On the hindwing underside, what best des: Equidistant — spot 6 roughly midway between spot 5 and the end-cell bar

q_alitaeus_s1.question: Is there a spot at the extreme base of forewing underside space 10 (just above vein 10, near the cell-end)?

question_link.text: extreme base of forewing underside space 10

q_alitaeus_s1.hint: Look for a small isolated spot right at the base of FW space 10, distinct from the regular postdiscal row. Species with this spot: pseudomuta, sintanga, alitaeus, denta. Species without: elopura, arianaga, ariana, aida, havilandi.

choices.label: Yes — spot present at extreme base of FW space 10
No — no spot at extreme base of FW space 10

q_alitaeus_s1_tail.question: Is the hindwing tail notably short, approximately 2.5 mm (rather than the typical 3.5–4.0 mm for the group)?
q_alitaeus_s1_tail.hint: [A. denta](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1192142&preferred_place_id=6734) is the only alitaeus-group member with a distinctly shortened tail (~2.5 mm). All others have tails of 3.5–4.0 mm.

choices.label: Yes — tail short, ~2.5 mm
No — tail of normal length, 3.5–4.0 mm

q_alitaeus_s1_color.question: Are the underside markings distinctly reddish-brown and strongly contrasting with the ground colour?
q_alitaeus_s1_color.hint:
[A. sintanga tani](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+sintanga) (Malay Peninsula) has a pale slatey-purple underside with markings that are distinctly reddish-brown and strongly contrasting. [A. sintanga sintanga](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+sintanga) (Borneo) has a crimson ground colour — even more distinctive.
[A. alitaeus mirabella](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=528441&preferred_place_id=6734) (Malay Peninsula, Langkawi) also has a purplish glaze, but its markings are purple-brown — NOT reddish-brown — and tend to be annular or macular.
[A. pseudomuta pseudomuta](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=498185&preferred_place_id=6734) is hair-brown with faint markings; [A. pseudomuta ariavana](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=498185&preferred_place_id=6734) (Langkawi) may show a purple wash but markings remain faint.
Answer Yes only if the markings are clearly reddish-brown and strongly contrasting.

choices.label: Yes — markings distinctly reddish-brown, strongly contrasting with pale slatey-purple ground
No — markings purple-brown, faint, or not strongly reddish-brown

q_alitaeus_s1_marks.question: Are the underside markings clearly visible and annular (ring-like), strongly contrasting with the ground colour?
q_alitaeus_s1_marks.hint:
(per field-photo review) HW postdiscal spot 6 is touching/overlapping the end-cell bar in both species and is no longer a useful discriminator here — use the markings instead.
[A. alitaeus](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=528441&preferred_place_id=6734): markings purple-brown, annular/macular, clearly contrasting with the pale slatey-purple ground colour; HW postdiscal spot 6 rounded, touching/overlapping the end-cell bar; spots 5, 6 and 7 widely out of line; HW postdiscal band completely dislocated at vein 2.
[A. pseudomuta](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=498185&preferred_place_id=6734): markings faint, hair-brown, barely darker than the ground colour; HW postdiscal spot 6 directly below spot 7, also touching/overlapping the end-cell bar, outer edge curved outward (convex); spots 5, 6 and 7 also widely out of line; HW postdiscal band completely dislocated at vein 2.

choices.label: Yes — markings annular and clearly contrasting with the ground colour
No — markings faint, barely darker than ground colour

q_alitaeus_s2_color.question: Is the underside ground colour overall pale — slatey-purple with strongly contrasting reddish-brown markings?
q_alitaeus_s2_color.hint: [A. havilandi](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1168050&preferred_place_id=6734) has a pale slatey-purple ground colour with strongly contrasting reddish-brown markings and a thread-like black border on the male upperside. If the underside is hair-brown or dark brownish, choose No.

choices.label: Yes — pale slatey purple with contrasting reddish-brown markings
No — hair-brown or dark brownish underside

q_alitaeus_s2_glaze.question: Is the underside ground colour overall dark brownish (not hair-brown), with only a subtle slatey or purplish glaze over the dark brown — not an overall pale ground colour?
q_alitaeus_s2_glaze.hint: [A. ariana](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=816304&preferred_place_id=6734) is dark brown with a faint purplish wash; [A. aida](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=880740&preferred_place_id=6734) is brown with a slatey to purplish glaze and a slightly produced FW apex. [A. elopura](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=880746&preferred_place_id=6734) and [A. arianaga](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1556287&preferred_place_id=6734) are hair-brown with markings barely darker than the ground colour.

choices.label: Yes — dark brownish with a slatey or purplish glaze
No — hair-brown, markings barely darker than ground colour

q_alitaeus_s2_term.question: Is the forewing termen notably straight and the apex more produced (pointed) than other group members?
q_alitaeus_s2_term.hint:
Both [A. aida](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=880740&preferred_place_id=6734) and [A. ariana](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=816304&preferred_place_id=6734) have postdiscal spot 4 shifted distad out of line with spots 5 and 6 — this is NOT the distinguishing character here.

[A. aida](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=880740&preferred_place_id=6734): FW termen distinctly straight and the apex more produced than other alitaeus-group members; HW postdiscal band completely dislocated at vein 2; male black border broad, expanding from ~1.25 mm at the tornus to ~2.0 mm at the apex.
[A. ariana](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=816304&preferred_place_id=6734): FW shape more normal (termen less straight, apex less produced); male border 1.0–1.5 mm, slightly wider at apex.

choices.label: Yes — FW termen straight; apex more produced; male border widens to ~2.0 mm at apex
No — FW shape more normal; apex less produced; male border 1.0–1.5 mm

q_alitaeus_s2_sp6.question: Is the hindwing postdiscal space 6 partly fuscous (darkened)?
q_alitaeus_s2_sp6.hint: [A. elopura](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=880746&preferred_place_id=6734) has HW space 6 partly fuscous (dark). In [A. arianaga](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1556287&preferred_place_id=6734), HW space 6 is entirely blue; underside markings are slightly darker than the ground colour with a faint pinkish glaze, and the male border is reduced to a thread.

choices.label: Yes — HW space 6 partly fuscous; male upperside purer blue, border ~0.5 mm
No — HW space 6 entirely blue; faint pinkish glaze; male border thread-like

r_pseudomuta.name: Arhopala pseudomuta
r_pseudomuta.note: Underside hair-brown; markings hardly darker than ground colour. FW: basal spot present in space 10. HW: postdiscal spot 6 directly below spot 7, touching/overlapping the end-cell bar; outer edge curved outward (convex) (per field-photo review — supersedes the earlier 'slightly oblique, does not overlap' literature description). Male: violet-blue with black border ~1.0 mm. Tail ~3.5–4.0 mm.

r_elopura.name: Arhopala elopura
r_elopura.note: Underside ground colour uniform hair brown; markings notably faint, barely darker than the ground colour (less contrasting than [A. pseudomuta](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=498185&preferred_place_id=6734)). FW: costal spot in mid-space 11 above the central cell spot; postdiscal spot 4 shifted distad, out of line with spots 5 and 6; no spot at extreme base of space 10. HW: postdiscal band completely dislocated at vein 2; postdiscal space 6 partly fuscous; postdiscal spot 6 outwardly straight or convex — inner edge in line with or inside the inner edge of spot 7, touching or overlapping the end-cell bar; centres of spots 5, 6 and 7 widely out of line. Tail ~3.5–4.0 mm; FW 17–19 mm. Male: shining blue; black border very narrow, thread-like to just under 0.5 mm at apex. Female: broader black borders; blue areas often a purer shade than in related species.

features.On the hindwing underside, is the postdi: No — spot 6 displaced past midway; touching or overlapping the end-cell bar; spots 5, 6 and 7 widely out of line

r_arianaga.name: Arhopala arianaga
r_arianaga.note: Underside markings slightly darker than ground colour with a very faint pinkish glaze. FW: no basal spot in space 10. HW: postdiscal space 6 entirely blue. Male: black border reduced to a thread. Tail ~3.5–4.0 mm.

features.On the hindwing underside, is the postdi: No — spot 6 displaced past midway; touching or overlapping the end-cell bar; spots 5, 6 and 7 widely out of line

r_ariana.name: Arhopala ariana
r_ariana.note: Underside dark brown with a faint purplish wash. FW: no basal spot in space 10; postdiscal spot in space 4 shifted distad out of line. Male: border 1.0–1.5 mm, slightly wider at apex. Tail ~3.5–4.0 mm.

features.On the hindwing underside, is the postdi: No — spot 6 displaced past midway; touching or overlapping the end-cell bar; spots 5, 6 and 7 widely out of line

r_aida.name: Arhopala aida
r_aida.note:
Underside paler brown. Nominate subspecies ([A. aida aida](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=880740&preferred_place_id=6734)): slatey grey glaze — strong in Langkawi specimens, weaker through the rest of the Malay Peninsula. [A. aida ophir](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=880740&preferred_place_id=6734) (Mt. Ophir): pale purple glaze instead.

FW: no basal spot in space 10; costal spot in mid-space 11; postdiscal spot 4 shifted distad out of line with spots 5 and 6; termen straight and more produced than other alitaeus-group members. HW: postdiscal spot 6 outwardly straight or convex; postdiscal band completely dislocated at vein 2. Male: black borders relatively broad, expanding from ~1.25 mm at tornus to ~2.0 mm at apex. Tail ~3.5–4.0 mm. FW 16.0–19.0 mm.

features.On the hindwing underside, is the postdi: No — spot 6 displaced past midway; touching or overlapping the end-cell bar; spots 5, 6 and 7 widely out of line

r_havilandi.name: Arhopala havilandi
r_havilandi.note: Underside pale slatey purple with contrasting reddish-brown markings. FW: no basal spot in space 10. Male: violet-blue, black border reduced to a thread. Tail ~3.5–4.0 mm.

features.On the hindwing underside, is the postdi: No — spot 6 displaced past midway; touching or overlapping the end-cell bar; spots 5, 6 and 7 widely out of line

r_sintanga.name: Arhopala sintanga
r_sintanga.note:
⚠ Extremely rare — no confirmed iNaturalist records. Verify carefully before confirming this identification.

Two subspecies:
• [A. sintanga tani](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+sintanga) (Malay Peninsula): pale slatey purple underside with contrasting reddish-brown markings; deeper violet upperside. Distinguished from the similar [A. havilandi kota](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1168050&preferred_place_id=6734) by the spot at the extreme base of FW space 10 (havilandi lacks this spot).
• [A. sintanga sintanga](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+sintanga) (Borneo): nominate subspecies; underside ground colour crimson (highly distinctive); wider black borders on upperside.

Underside characters (both subspecies): spot at extreme base of FW space 10; central spot in FW space 11; FW postdiscal spot 4 shifted distad; HW spot 6 touches or overlaps the end-cell bar; spots 5, 6 and 7 widely out of line; HW postdiscal band completely dislocated at vein 2. Tail 3.5–4.0 mm.

features.On the hindwing underside, is the postdi: No — spot 6 displaced past midway; touching or overlapping the end-cell bar; spots 5, 6 and 7 widely out of line

r_alitaeus.name: Arhopala alitaeus
r_alitaeus.note: Underside markings notably annular (ring-like); FW postdiscal spot in space 1b often dislocated outward from spot in space 2. FW: basal spot present in space 10. Male: regular black borders ≥1.0 mm wide. Tail ~3.5–4.0 mm.

features.On the hindwing underside, is the postdi: No — spot 6 displaced past midway; touching or overlapping the end-cell bar; spots 5, 6 and 7 widely out of line

r_denta.name: Arhopala denta
r_denta.note: Distinctive SHORT tail — only ~2.5 mm (others in the group have 3.5–4.0 mm). FW: basal spot present in space 10. Underside hair-brown with at most a slight pinkish glaze. Male: brilliant blue, black border reduced to a thread.

features.On the hindwing underside, is the postdi: No — spot 6 displaced past midway; touching or overlapping the end-cell bar; spots 5, 6 and 7 widely out of line

r_kurzi.name: Arhopala kurzi
r_kurzi.note: Tailless; HW tornus very rounded. FW 18–22 mm. Male: rather dull purple-blue with a thread-like black border. Female: purple; HW border unusually variable — purple sometimes reaches termen between veins 2 and 5, other specimens almost confined to cell. Underside FW: markings variable; lower postdiscal band sometimes obsolete; when fully developed, spot in space 1b shifted inward, placed midway between end-cell bar and postdiscal spot in space 2. Underside HW: postdiscal band WIDELY dislocated at vein 2 (diagnostic for subgroup). Forest species, low to moderate elevations.

features.On the forewing underside, is the basal: No — basal cell spot present on FW underside
features.Is there a distinct white patch on the h: No — no white patch on HW cilia

q_amph_s2.question: On the forewing underside, is the basal cell spot absent?
q_amph_s2.hint: [A. avathina avathina](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=888416&preferred_place_id=6734) is recognized by the absence of the basal cell spot on the FW underside; additionally, the HW postdiscal band is completely dislocated at vein 2. All other amphimuta subgroup members have a basal cell spot.

choices.label: Yes — no basal cell spot on FW underside (HW band also completely dislocated at vein 2)
No — basal cell spot present on FW underside

q_amph_s3.question: Is there a distinct white patch on the hindwing cilia just above the tornal lobe?
q_amph_s3.hint: [A. norda](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=887831&preferred_place_id=6734) has a small but distinctive white patch on the HW cilia just above the tornal lobe, and also has less rounded (more angular) postdiscal spots compared to [A. major](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=429756&preferred_place_id=6734).

choices.label: Yes — white patch on HW cilia above tornal lobe; postdiscal spots less rounded
No — no white patch on HW cilia

q_amph_s4.question: Is the male's black border broad, 1.5 mm or wider?
q_amph_s4.hint: [A. major](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=429756&preferred_place_id=6734) has a border expanding from 2.0 mm at tornus to 3.0 mm at apex (blue upperside). [A. stubbsi](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+stubbsi) has a broad ~2.0 mm border but is dull bluish purple. All others have narrow borders (0.5 mm or thread-like). If the upperside is not visible, select Cannot determine — the key will continue using underside characters.

choices.label: Yes — border ≥1.5 mm wide
No — border narrow (0.5 mm or thread-like)
Cannot determine — upperside not visible

q_amph_broad.question: Is the male distinctly blue above, with the border widening from ~2.0 mm at the tornus to ~3.0 mm at the apex?
q_amph_broad.hint: [A. major major](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=429756&preferred_place_id=6734) is bluer than [A. amphimuta](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=498183&preferred_place_id=6734); its border expands toward the apex. [A. stubbsi](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+stubbsi) is dull bluish purple with a broad but even ~2.0 mm border. If the upperside is not visible, select Cannot determine — [A. major](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=429756&preferred_place_id=6734) and [A. stubbsi](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+stubbsi) cannot be reliably separated from the underside alone.

choices.label: Yes — distinctly blue; border expands 2.0→3.0 mm from tornus to apex
No — dull bluish purple; border broad but even (~2.0 mm)
Cannot determine — upperside not visible

q_amph_s5.question: Is the male's black border reduced to a thread (very narrow, well under 0.5 mm)?
q_amph_s5.hint: Peninsula thread-border species: [A. moolaiana yajuna](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=549326&preferred_place_id=6734) (shining violet-blue; female blue reaches HW termen). Bornean thread-border species ([A. alica](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1495881&preferred_place_id=6734)) have already been separated by the geography filter. Regular 0.5 mm border Peninsula species: [A. amphimuta](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=498183&preferred_place_id=6734), [A. sceva](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=888422&preferred_place_id=6734), [A. zylda](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1340244&preferred_place_id=6734). If the upperside is not visible, select Cannot determine — the key will continue using underside characters.

choices.label: Yes — border thread-like
No — border regular ~0.5 mm
Cannot determine — upperside not visible

q_amph_cilia_spot1a.question: On the hindwing underside, is there a small white spot on the cilia near the tornus together with a very weak tooth at vein 2 — and if present, are the postdiscal spots more rounded or less rounded (more angular, like [A. norda](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=887831&preferred_place_id=6734))?

question_link.text: weak tooth at vein 2

q_amph_cilia_spot1a.hint: [A. moolaiana yajuna](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=549326&preferred_place_id=6734), [A. amphimuta](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=498183&preferred_place_id=6734) and [A. major](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=429756&preferred_place_id=6734) all have a small white spot on the HW cilia near the tornus with a very weak tooth at vein 2 (HW postdiscal band only partially dislocated at vein 2). [A. moolaiana](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=549326&preferred_place_id=6734)'s postdiscal spots are less rounded — like [A. norda](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=887831&preferred_place_id=6734) — while [A. amphimuta](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=498183&preferred_place_id=6734) and [A. major](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=429756&preferred_place_id=6734) have more rounded spots and are themselves indistinguishable beneath. [A. zylda](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1340244&preferred_place_id=6734) and [A. stubbsi](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+stubbsi) lack the white cilia spot and tooth altogether and are separated from each other only by upperside characters (border colour and width).

choices.label: Yes — white cilia spot near the tornus + weak tooth present; postdiscal spots less rounded (more angular), similar to A. norda
Yes — white cilia spot near the tornus + weak tooth present; postdiscal spots more rounded, similar to A. amphimuta/A. major
No — no white cilia spot or tooth at vein 2 (underside like A. major/A. moolaiana, separated by upperside)

q_amph_s6.question: Is the hindwing underside postdiscal series dislocated at vein 2, and is the species noticeably smaller than its allies?
q_amph_s6.hint: [A. sceva indra](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=888422&preferred_place_id=6734) has the HW postdiscal series clearly dislocated at vein 2 and is a smaller species; male is dark violet-blue. In the remaining Peninsula species (amphimuta, zylda), the HW band is not strongly dislocated at vein 2.

choices.label: Yes — HW postdiscal series dislocated at vein 2; smaller species; male dark violet-blue
No — HW band not strongly dislocated at vein 2

q_amph_s7.question: Is the male a warm violet-purple (with a reddish hue rather than blue), and is the FW postdiscal spot in space 3 an oblique oval?
q_amph_s7.hint: [A. amphimuta amphimuta](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=498183&preferred_place_id=6734) is violet-purple (warmer/redder than violet-blue) with the FW spot 3 usually an oblique oval, and the FW band slightly dislocated at vein 4. FW length 20–23 mm. [A. zylda elioti](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1340244&preferred_place_id=6734) is violet-blue, smaller (FW 16–17 mm), with female exceptionally narrow borders. Both are Peninsula species. Note: FW space 3 is usually hidden under the hindwing — the warm vs blue upperside colour and FW size are the more reliable characters. If the upperside is not visible, use the 'Cannot determine' option.

choices.label: Yes — warm violet-purple; FW spot 3 oblique oval; FW band slightly dislocated at vein 4
No — violet-blue (not warm purple); smaller (FW 16–17 mm); female exceptionally narrow-bordered
Distinctly blue (not violet or purple) with a broad border ≥2 mm — may be A. major or A. stubbsi
Cannot determine — upperside not visible

q_amph_s7_cd.question: What is the forewing length, and are the hindwing cilia prolonged into a short tooth at vein 2?
q_amph_s7_cd.hint: When the upperside is not visible, FW size and the HW cilia tooth are the best underside characters. [A. amphimuta amphimuta](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=498183&preferred_place_id=6734) is larger (FW 20–23 mm) and often has a short tooth-like prolongation of the cilia at vein 2 (may be inconspicuous in worn specimens). [A. zylda elioti](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1340244&preferred_place_id=6734) is smaller (FW 16–17 mm) with evenly rounded HW cilia and no tooth at vein 2.

choices.label: Larger (FW ~20–23 mm); HW cilia often form a short tooth at vein 2
Smaller (FW ~16–17 mm); HW cilia evenly rounded, no tooth at vein 2
Cannot determine — size unclear and cilia tooth not visible

q_amph_geo.question: Is the specimen from Borneo (not the Malay Peninsula)?
q_amph_geo.hint: Three amphimuta subgroup members are Bornean endemics: [A. alica](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1495881&preferred_place_id=6734) (thread-like border), [A. baluensis](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=888417&preferred_place_id=6734) (lacks white spot in HW cilia space 1a), and [A. dajagaka](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+dajagaka) (female has thick borders and darker venation). The remaining 6 species at this point are Malay Peninsula species.

choices.label: Yes — Bornean specimen
No — Malay Peninsula specimen

q_amph_colour.question: What is the male's overall upperside colour?
q_amph_colour.hint: [A. major](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=429756&preferred_place_id=6734) is the brightest: cool, saturated blue with no violet tone. [A. stubbsi](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+stubbsi) is dull bluish-purple (blue still dominates). Both have the broad ≥1.5 mm border. All others have a violet component: [A. amphimuta](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=498183&preferred_place_id=6734) is warm violet-purple (reddish); [A. zylda](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1340244&preferred_place_id=6734) and [A. sceva](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=888422&preferred_place_id=6734) are violet-blue (cooler, but clearly violet); [A. moolaiana](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=549326&preferred_place_id=6734) is shining violet-blue. If the upperside is not visible, choose Cannot determine — the key continues using border width and underside characters.

choices.label: Distinctly blue or bluish-purple — cool tone, no warm reddish-violet hue (A. major / A. stubbsi)
Violet-blue or violet-purple — clear warm or violet tone (A. amphimuta / A. zylda / A. moolaiana / A. sceva)
Cannot determine — upperside not visible

q_amph_bor3.question: Is the male's black border reduced to a thread (very narrow), and is the male a dark, dull purple?
q_amph_bor3.hint: [A. alica](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1495881&preferred_place_id=6734): thread-like border; male dark dull purple (distinctly duller than [A. moolaiana](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=549326&preferred_place_id=6734)'s shining violet-blue); FW ~17 mm; female purple with broad black borders. [A. baluensis](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=888417&preferred_place_id=6734) and [A. dajagaka](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+dajagaka) both have a regular ~0.5 mm border and are dark purple-blue. If the upperside is not visible, select Cannot determine — the next question uses wing size instead.

choices.label: Yes — border thread-like; male dark dull purple; FW ~17 mm; female purple, broad borders
No — border regular ~0.5 mm; male dark purple-blue
Cannot determine — upperside not visible

q_amph_bor2.question: Is the specimen large (FW approximately 21–25 mm), or does the female have thick black borders with darker venation?
q_amph_bor2.hint: Size is the most accessible separator: [A. dajagaka](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+dajagaka) is distinctly larger (FW 21–25 mm); [A. baluensis](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=888417&preferred_place_id=6734) is smaller (FW 18–20 mm). Confirm with female characters: [A. dajagaka](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+dajagaka) female has thick black borders and noticeably darker venation. [A. baluensis](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=888417&preferred_place_id=6734) female has narrower-than-typical borders; HW cilia in space 1a lacks a white spot (rarely obscurely present). Note: both have male valva unilobate; genitalia dissection gives certain identification.

choices.label: Larger (FW 21–25 mm); female with thick black borders and darker venation
Smaller (FW 18–20 mm); female with narrower borders; HW cilia space 1a lacks white spot
Very small (FW ~17 mm) — smaller than A. baluensis

r_delta.name: Arhopala delta
r_delta.note: Tailed — distinct tail at vein 2; the ONLY tailed member of the amphimuta subgroup (all others tailless). Male: deep violet with a narrow, regular black border. Underside: FW postdiscal band nearly completely dislocated at vein 4. HW postdiscal spots in spaces 6 and 7 more or less rounded; spots 5–7 in echelon (centres in straight line).

features.On the forewing underside, is the basal: No — basal cell spot present on FW underside
features.Is there a distinct white patch on the h: No — no white patch on HW cilia
features.What best describes the forewing postdis: HW postdiscal spots 5, 6, and 7 overlap with their centres forming a straight line (echelon)
features.Is the forewing postdiscal band nearly c: Yes — FW band nearly completely dislocated at vein 4
features.Are the underside postdiscal spots unifo: No — underside spots not uniformly narrow rectangular (eumolphus group, rounded or acute apex)

r_avathina_avathina.name: Arhopala avathina avathina
r_avathina_avathina.note: Tailless. Distinctive: FW underside lacks a basal cell spot (absent, not obscure). HW postdiscal band completely dislocated at vein 2. Otherwise similar to other amphimuta subgroup members.

r_norda.name: Arhopala norda
r_norda.note: Tailless. Distinguished from [A. major](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=429756&preferred_place_id=6734) by the small white patch on the HW cilia just above the tornal lobe, and by less rounded (more angular) postdiscal spots on the underside.

r_major_major.name: Arhopala major major
r_major_major.note: Tailless. Both sexes bluer than [A. amphimuta](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=498183&preferred_place_id=6734) above. Male: FW black border expands from 2.0 mm at tornus to 3.0 mm at apex. Underside similar to [A. amphimuta](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=498183&preferred_place_id=6734). HW cilia often prolonged into a short tooth at vein 2.

features.Is the hindwing underside postdiscal ser: No — HW band not strongly dislocated at vein 2
features.On the hindwing underside, is there a sm: Yes — white cilia spot near the tornus + weak tooth present; postdiscal spots more rounded, similar to A. amphimuta/A. major
features.Is there a distinct white patch on the h: Cannot determine — duplicate of the cilia-spot/tooth question above; A. major does have the spot (canonical path answer is an artifact of routing to A. norda)

r_stubbsi.name: Arhopala stubbsi
r_stubbsi.note: Tailless. Male: rather dull bluish-purple with a broad, even ~2.0 mm black border — the widest border in the amphimuta subgroup. HW cilia often prolonged at vein 2.

features.Is the hindwing underside postdiscal ser: No — HW band not strongly dislocated at vein 2
features.On the hindwing underside, is there a sm: No — no white cilia spot or tooth at vein 2 (underside like A. major/A. moolaiana, separated by upperside)
features.Is there a distinct white patch on the h: Cannot determine — duplicate of the cilia-spot/tooth question above; A. stubbsi correctly lacks the spot, already established there

r_moolaiana_yajuna.name: Arhopala moolaiana yajuna
r_moolaiana_yajuna.note: Tailless. Male: bright shining violet-blue with a thread-like black border; FW 18–22 mm. Female: blue colour reaches the HW termen or very nearly so — black border is extremely narrow or absent at the termen (diagnostic; unique in the amphimuta group). Underside markings very similar to [A. norda](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=887831&preferred_place_id=6734). HW postdiscal band only partially dislocated at vein 2. Range: Malay Peninsula and south Myanmar. (Feature Scoring: Q102 = 'No — HW band not strongly dislocated at vein 2'; the key tree reaches this species via Q101 'Yes — border thread-like' as a routing shortcut, bypassing Q102.)

features.Is the hindwing underside postdiscal ser: No — HW band not strongly dislocated at vein 2
features.Is there a distinct white patch on the h: Cannot determine — duplicate of the cilia-spot/tooth question below; A. moolaiana does have the spot (already established there, confirmed by field photo)

r_alica.name: Arhopala alica
r_alica.note: Tailless. Bornean endemic. Male: dark, dull purple with a thread-like black border; FW ~17 mm. Female: purple with broad black borders (contrasts sharply with [A. moolaiana](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=549326&preferred_place_id=6734) female). HW postdiscal band only partially dislocated at vein 2. Distinguished from [A. moolaiana](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=549326&preferred_place_id=6734) by: darker/duller male colour, smaller size, broader female borders, and Bornean range.

features.On the hindwing underside, what best des: Cannot determine — HW spot 6 position unclear

r_sceva_indra.name: Arhopala sceva indra
r_sceva_indra.note: Tailless. A smaller species. HW underside postdiscal series is dislocated at vein 2 (distinctive within the amphimuta subgroup). Male: dark violet-blue.

r_amphimuta_amphimuta.name: Arhopala amphimuta amphimuta
r_amphimuta_amphimuta.note: Tailless. Male: dark bluish-purple with a regular 0.5–1.0 mm black border; FW 20–23 mm. HW cilia often prolonged into a very short tooth at vein 2 (may be inconspicuous in worn specimens). FW underside: postdiscal band slightly dislocated at vein 4; spot in space 3 usually an oblique oval. Female: lighter purple with typical broad black borders. HW postdiscal band only partially dislocated at vein 2 (as in [A. zylda](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1340244&preferred_place_id=6734)).

features.Is the hindwing underside postdiscal ser: No — HW band not strongly dislocated at vein 2
features.On the hindwing underside, is there a sm: Yes — white cilia spot near the tornus + weak tooth present; postdiscal spots more rounded, similar to A. amphimuta/A. major
features.Is there a distinct white patch on the h: Cannot determine — duplicate of the cilia-spot/tooth question above; A. amphimuta does have the spot (canonical path answer is an artifact of routing to A. norda)

r_zylda_elioti.name: Arhopala zylda elioti
r_zylda_elioti.note: Tailless. Male: shining blue with a slight violet tinge; border 1.0 mm; FW 16–17 mm. HW evenly rounded — no trace of a tooth at vein 2 ([A. amphimuta](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=498183&preferred_place_id=6734) has a short tooth). Female: paler blue with exceptionally narrow black borders (the narrowest in the amphimuta subgroup). HW postdiscal band only partially dislocated at vein 2 (as in [A. amphimuta](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=498183&preferred_place_id=6734)). Malay Peninsula.

features.Is the hindwing underside postdiscal ser: No — HW band not strongly dislocated at vein 2
features.On the hindwing underside, is there a sm: No — no white cilia spot or tooth at vein 2 (underside like A. major/A. moolaiana, separated by upperside)
features.Is there a distinct white patch on the h: Cannot determine — duplicate of the cilia-spot/tooth question above; A. zylda correctly lacks the spot, already established there

r_dajagaka.name: Arhopala dajagaka
r_dajagaka.note: Tailless. Bornean endemic. Larger Bornean species (FW 21–25 mm). Male: dark bluish-purple with a thin black border. Female: THICK black borders and noticeably darker venation across the wings (diagnostic). Male valva unilobate; certain identification may require genitalia dissection.

r_baluensis.name: Arhopala baluensis
r_baluensis.note: Tailless. Bornean endemic. Smaller Bornean species (FW 18–20 mm). Male: dark purple-blue with a regular 0.5 mm border. Female: purple-blue with narrower-than-typical black borders. HW cilia in space 1a lacks a white spot (may be rarely present but obscure). Male valva unilobate; certain identification may require genitalia dissection.

q_muta_size.question: What is the forewing length?
q_muta_size.hint: [A. tropaea](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=785409&preferred_place_id=6734) is notably small (FW 13.0–14.0 mm) — distinctly smaller than [A. moorei busa](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=780493&preferred_place_id=6734) (FW 16.0–18.0 mm) and [A. muta maranda](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=780491&preferred_place_id=6734) (FW 17.0–19.0 mm). Forewing length alone separates [A. tropaea](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=785409&preferred_place_id=6734) from the other two muta-subgroup species, even when the upperside cannot be examined.

choices.label: Small — FW 13.0–14.0 mm
Larger — FW 16.0–19.0 mm
Cannot determine — forewing length not measured

q_muta_s1.question: Is the species noticeably small and duller bluish-purple on the male upperside?
q_muta_s1.hint: [A. tropaea](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=785409&preferred_place_id=6734) is distinctly smaller (FW 13.0–14.0 mm) than [A. moorei busa](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=780493&preferred_place_id=6734) (FW 16.0–18.0 mm) and [A. muta maranda](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=780491&preferred_place_id=6734) (FW 17.0–19.0 mm), with a duller, less saturated bluish-purple male upperside. [A. muta](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=780491&preferred_place_id=6734) and [A. moorei](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=780493&preferred_place_id=6734) are larger, brighter blue.

choices.label: Yes — noticeably small; male duller bluish-purple
No — larger (FW 16.0–19.0 mm); male bright blue
Cannot determine — upperside not visible

q_muta_s2.question: On the male upperside, are both the forewing and hindwing exactly the same shade of blue in all lights?
q_muta_s2.hint: [A. moorei busa](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=780493&preferred_place_id=6734): both wings the same shade of blue (bluish-purple) in all lights; female is duller and more purple than [A. muta](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=780491&preferred_place_id=6734)'s female; FW 16.0–18.0 mm. [A. muta maranda](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=780491&preferred_place_id=6734): FW is a rather dark, bright blue; HW is slightly paler and more glistening, so the two wings are NOT the same shade in all lights; FW border expands from 1.0–1.5 mm at tornus to 1.5–2.0 mm at apex; FW 17.0–19.0 mm.

choices.label: Yes — both wings same shade of blue in all lights; female more purple (FW 16.0–18.0 mm)
No — FW dark bright blue; HW slightly paler and more glistening; border expands toward apex (FW 17.0–19.0 mm)
Cannot determine — upperside not visible

r_tropaea.name: Arhopala tropaea
r_tropaea.note: Tailless. Very small species (FW 13.0–14.0 mm). Male: duller bluish-purple upperside (noticeably less bright than [A. muta](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=780491&preferred_place_id=6734) or [A. moorei](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=780493&preferred_place_id=6734)). Underside: postdiscal spots quadrate/banded; FW space 2 spot stepped toward termen (muta subgroup).

features.On the hindwing underside, what best des: Not displaced / Slightly displaced — spot 6 roughly level with spot 5, or only minimally shifted inward; spots 5, 6 and 7 broadly in line

r_moorei_busa.name: Arhopala moorei busa
r_moorei_busa.note: Tailless. Male: both wings the same shade of blue in all lights — key distinction from [A. muta maranda](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=780491&preferred_place_id=6734), whose HW is slightly paler and brighter (FW 16.0–18.0 mm). Female: bluish purple with broad black borders (duller and more purple than [A. muta](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=780491&preferred_place_id=6734)'s female). Underside: spots quadrate/banded; FW space 2 spot stepped toward termen; HW postdiscal band dislocated — inner edge of spot in space 2 in line with, or only slightly outside, the outer edge of spot 1b. HW cilia: white spot in space 1a absent (very rarely present but extremely obscure).

features.On the hindwing underside, what best des: Equidistant — spot 6 roughly midway between spot 5 and the end-cell bar
features.Is the HW postdiscal band only partially: Yes — band only partially dislocated at vein 2 (shifted but connected)
features.Are the hindwing underside postdiscal sp: Yes — HW spots more quadrate or banded in shape (muta subgroup)
features.On the hindwing underside, how large are: Normal — spots normal-sized, with a clear gap from the end-cell bar

r_muta_maranda.name: Arhopala muta maranda
r_muta_maranda.note: Tailless. Male: FW rather dark bright blue; HW slightly paler and more glistening — the two wings are NOT the same shade in all lights (FW 17.0–19.0 mm). FW black border expands from 1.0–1.5 mm at tornus to 1.5–2.0 mm at apex. Female: shining blue with only a slight purple tint, broad black borders. Underside: spots quadrate/banded; FW space 2 spot stepped toward termen; HW postdiscal band dislocated — inner edge of spot in space 2 in line with, or only slightly outside, the outer edge of spot 1b. HW cilia: white spot in space 1a absent.

features.On the hindwing underside, what best des: Not displaced / Slightly displaced — spot 6 roughly level with spot 5, or only minimally shifted inward; spots 5, 6 and 7 broadly in line
features.On the hindwing underside, how large are: Normal — spots normal-sized, with a clear gap from the end-cell bar

q_perimuta_s1.question: Is there a yellowish discal area on the hindwing underside with obscure markings and no tornal green scales?
q_perimuta_s1.hint: [A. perimuta regina](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=448212&preferred_place_id=6734) is easily recognized by the yellowish (not white or grey) discal area on the HW underside, markings obscure or almost absent, and tornal green scales completely absent. If HW underside is normal, continue to the next question.

choices.label: Yes — yellowish HW discal area; markings obscure; no tornal green scales
No — HW underside normal

q_perimuta_s2.question: Is the male hindwing upperside a brilliant greenish-blue, contrasting strongly with the dark shining violet-blue forewing?
q_perimuta_s2.hint: [A. metamuta metamuta](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1340245&preferred_place_id=6734) has a striking two-tone male: dark shining violet-blue FW (1.5 mm border) vs. brilliant greenish-blue HW — the most visually distinctive species in the perimuta subgroup. [A. antimuta antimuta](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=498184&preferred_place_id=6734) (the most abundant amphimuta-group species) lacks this contrast: male is uniformly deep violet-blue with the border reduced to a thread; HW underside spot 6 is placed below spot 7.

choices.label: Yes — HW brilliant greenish-blue, contrasting with dark shining violet-blue FW
No — uniformly deep violet-blue; border thread-like
Cannot determine — upperside not visible

r_perimuta_regina.name: Arhopala perimuta regina
r_perimuta_regina.note: Tailless; evenly rounded HW tornus with no tooth or tail at vein 2. Easily recognized: HW underside has a prominent central yellowish discal area; markings generally obscure or ill-defined; tornal green scales completely absent. HW postdiscal spots 5, 6, and 7 in echelon (centres overlapping, in a straight line); spot 6 positioned nearer to spot 5 than to the end-cell bar. FW: postdiscal spots 2 and 3 in line (may be oblique); spot 4 shifted distad out of line with spots 5 and 6; dark area under the FW cell obscure, ill-defined, or absent; no spot at the extreme base of space 3.

r_metamuta_metamuta.name: Arhopala metamuta metamuta
r_metamuta_metamuta.note:
⚠ Extremely rare — verify carefully before confirming this identification.

Tailless; evenly rounded HW tornus with no tooth or tail at vein 2. Striking upperside: male has a brilliant greenish-blue HW that contrasts strongly with the dark, shining violet-blue FW (1.5 mm border). FW postdiscal spots 2 and 3 are in line (perimuta subgroup). If any doubt remains about the contrasting HW colour, reconsider [A. antimuta antimuta](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=498184&preferred_place_id=6734) (the far more abundant perimuta-subgroup species).

r_antimuta_antimuta.name: Arhopala antimuta antimuta
r_antimuta_antimuta.note: The most abundant species in the amphimuta group. Tailless. FW ~16.0 mm. Male: deep violet-blue; black border reduced to a thread. HW underside: postdiscal band completely dislocated at vein 2 (though this character can sometimes be inconstant — cf. [A. arvina adalitas](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1294761&preferred_place_id=6734), in which it is only partially dislocated); postdiscal spot 6 positioned directly below spot 7, displaced well past the postdiscal row — touching or overlapping the end-cell bar; spots 5, 6 and 7 widely out of line. FW postdiscal spots 2 and 3 are in line (perimuta subgroup). Confirmatory: male upperside uniformly coloured — no contrasting wing colour as in [A. metamuta](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1340245&preferred_place_id=6734).

features.On the hindwing underside, what best des: Spot 6 touching or overlapping the end-cell bar; or spots 5, 6 and 7 widely out of line / displaced

q_anthelus_s1.question: Does the forewing underside have at least 3 spots in space 11, AND does the hindwing underside have 2 subbasal spots in space 8?

question_link.text: 3 spots in space 11

q_anthelus_s1.hint: [A. anthelus grahami](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=149211&preferred_place_id=6734) (anthelus subgroup): EXTRA spots — ≥3 in FW space 11 and 2 subbasal spots in HW space 8; spots on FW and upper HW half are deep purple-brown; FW 28 mm. [A. achelous achelous](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=940963&preferred_place_id=6734) (achelous subgroup): NO spots in FW space 11 and only a single spot at the base of HW space 8; FW 22 mm. Additional diagnostic for achelous: HW postdiscal spot 6 is noticeably smaller than spot 7 — roughly a quarter its size (spots 5, 6 and 7 otherwise in the normal echelon arrangement, spot 6 nearer to spot 5 than to the end-cell bar). The nominate shows a strong purple tinge on the underside; subspecies malu (Kedawi) lacks most of this purple tinge.

choices.label: Yes — ≥3 spots in FW space 11; 2 subbasal spots in HW space 8; spots deep purple-brown
No — 0 spots in FW space 11; single spot at base of HW space 8

r_anthelus_grahami.name: Arhopala anthelus grahami
r_anthelus_grahami.note: One of the most beautiful in the genus. FW 28 mm. Underside: ≥3 spots in FW space 11; 2 spots at base of HW space 8; spots on FW and upper HW half deep purple-brown. Male: brilliant shining blue, narrow black border. Female: violet-blue with fairly broad black borders. Anthelus subgroup.

features.Is HW postdiscal spot 6 noticeably small: No — HW spot 6 not markedly smaller than spot 7; spots on upper HW half deep purple-brown

r_achelous_achelous.name: Arhopala achelous achelous
r_achelous_achelous.note: Tailed. FW 22 mm. Underside: strong purple tinge (nominate); no spots in FW space 11; single spot at base of HW space 8. Lacks spots in FW space 11 (cf. [A. anthelus](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=149211&preferred_place_id=6734)); single spot at base of HW space 8 (cf. two in [A. anthelus](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=149211&preferred_place_id=6734)). HW postdiscal spots 5, 6 and 7 in the normal echelon arrangement, spot 6 nearer to spot 5 than to the end-cell bar — but diagnostically, spot 6 is noticeably smaller than spot 7, roughly a quarter its size. Subspecies [A. achelous malu](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=940963&preferred_place_id=6734) (Kedawi: Langkawi, Perlis, north Kedah) lacks most of the purple tinge found in the nominate. Male: shining dark blue, narrow ~0.5 mm black border. Achelous subgroup.

features.Is HW postdiscal spot 6 noticeably small: Yes — HW spot 6 noticeably smaller than spot 7, roughly a quarter its size

q_aurea_s1.question: Is the underside glazed with a distinct purple wash?
q_aurea_s1.hint: [A. trogon](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=447353&preferred_place_id=6734) and [A. stinga](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1394352&preferred_place_id=6734) both have an underside glazed with a purple wash. [A. aurea](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=429759&preferred_place_id=6734), [A. caeca](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1607761&preferred_place_id=6734), and [A. borneensis](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=925156&preferred_place_id=6734) are hair-brown underneath (no purple glaze). This is the primary split within the aurea group.

choices.label: Yes — underside glazed with a purple wash
No — underside hair-brown (no purple glaze)

q_aurea_purple.question: Is the forewing black border significantly wide at the tornus — 2.5–3.0 mm?
q_aurea_purple.hint:
[A. stinga](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1394352&preferred_place_id=6734) has an unusually wide FW border of 2.5–3.0 mm at the tornus (the widest in the group), decreasing to a thread near the apex; the male has no basal blue suffusion. [A. trogon](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=447353&preferred_place_id=6734) has a thread-like border throughout; male is brilliant brassy green with a strong blue suffusion at the wing bases; HW green usually reaches the termen in spaces 3, 4, or 5.

If the upperside is not visible, select Cannot determine — both species share identical underside characters. Distribution can then help: [A. stinga](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1394352&preferred_place_id=6734) is restricted to Johor; [A. trogon](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=447353&preferred_place_id=6734) occurs throughout Peninsular Malaysia.

choices.label: Yes — FW border 2.5–3.0 mm at tornus; male green without blue base suffusion
No — FW border thread throughout; male brilliant brassy green with strong blue base suffusion
Cannot determine — upperside not visible; FW border and male coloration cannot be assessed

g_trogon_stinga_cd.group_name: A. trogon or A. stinga (upperside not visible)
g_trogon_stinga_cd.description: Without the upperside these two species cannot be separated — both share a purple-washed underside with identical patterning. Use distribution as a guide: [A. trogon](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=447353&preferred_place_id=6734) occurs throughout Peninsular Malaysia; [A. stinga](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1394352&preferred_place_id=6734) is restricted to Johor. If the observation is not from Johor, [A. trogon](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=447353&preferred_place_id=6734) is the more likely identification.
key_features: Underside glazed with a distinct purple wash
Short stout HW tail at vein 2 (just under 2 mm)
HW cell notably long — exceeds half the total wing length
Postdiscal spot 6 touches or overlaps the end-cell bar
g_trogon_stinga_cd.no_next_note: To confirm to species, the upperside forewing border width or male upperside coloration must be assessed.

q_aurea_brown.question: Are the underside markings incomplete — lacking basal/central cell spots on the forewing and various basal/discal spots on the hindwing, with a narrow postdiscal band?
q_aurea_brown.hint: [A. caeca](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1607761&preferred_place_id=6734) has distinctively incomplete underside markings (FW lacks basal and central cell spots; HW lacks various basal and discal spots; narrow postdiscal band) and the male green turns to violet-blue at the tornus of both wings. [A. aurea](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=429759&preferred_place_id=6734) and [A. borneensis](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=925156&preferred_place_id=6734) have complete markings.

choices.label: Yes — incomplete markings; male green turns to violet-blue at tornus of both wings
No — complete underside markings

q_aurea_brown2.question: In which region was this butterfly observed?
q_aurea_brown2.hint: [A. aurea](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=429759&preferred_place_id=6734) is recorded from Peninsular Malaysia and Singapore only — it is not found in Borneo. [A. borneensis](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=925156&preferred_place_id=6734) is a Bornean endemic (Sabah, Sarawak, Brunei, Kalimantan) and has not been recorded from the Malay Peninsula. These two species are allopatric, so locality alone is decisive here.

choices.label: Peninsular Malaysia or Singapore
Borneo (Sabah, Sarawak, Brunei, or Kalimantan)

r_aurea.name: Arhopala aurea
r_aurea.note: Tailed. Tail short and stout, just under 2.0 mm. Underside hair-brown with complete markings. FW postdiscal spot 4 shifted distad, out of line with spots 5 and 6; dark patch in FW space 1b not solid. HW postdiscal band partially dislocated at vein 2; postdiscal spot 6 widely out of line with spots 5 and 7 — inner edge of spot 6 in line with, or inside, the inner edge of spot 7; spot 6 touches or overlaps the end-cell bar. Male: HW cell considerably longer than half the wing; shining green with weak blue suffusion at wing bases; FW border 0.75–1.5 mm at tornus, decreasing to a thread above vein 3 or 4; HW green does not reach the termen. Female: blue to purple-blue with a relatively narrow HW border.

features.On the hindwing underside, is the postdi: No — spot 6 displaced past midway; touching or overlapping the end-cell bar; spots 5, 6 and 7 widely out of line
features.What best describes the forewing postdis: FW spot 4 out of line with others; HW spot 6 placed directly below spot 7 (spot 6 not sinuous)

r_trogon.name: Arhopala trogon
r_trogon.note: Underside glazed with a purple wash. Male: brilliant brassy green with a strong blue suffusion at the wing bases; FW border a thread throughout; HW green usually reaches the termen in spaces 3, 4, or 5. Short stumpy HW tail (~2 mm). HW end-cell bar notably long; postdiscal spot 6 touches or overlaps the end-cell bar. Distribution: occurs throughout Peninsular Malaysia, including Johor. [A. stinga](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1394352&preferred_place_id=6734) is restricted to Johor forest — outside Johor, A. trogon is far more likely; within Johor, both remain possible and upperside characters are needed to confirm.

features.On the hindwing underside, is the postdi: No — spot 6 displaced past midway; touching or overlapping the end-cell bar; spots 5, 6 and 7 widely out of line

r_stinga.name: Arhopala stinga
r_stinga.note: Underside glazed with a purple wash (exactly as in [A. trogon](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=447353&preferred_place_id=6734)). Male: green WITHOUT any basal blue suffusion (no blue at wing bases); FW border distinctively wide — 2.5–3.0 mm at tornus, decreasing to a thread near the apex (widest border in the group). Short stumpy HW tail just under 2 mm. HW cell notably long — exceeding half the total wing length. HW end-cell bar notably long; postdiscal spot 6 touches or overlaps the end-cell bar (spot 6 inner edge at or inside the inner edge of spot 7). Distribution: restricted to forest in Johor. Outside Johor, [A. trogon](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=447353&preferred_place_id=6734) (which occurs throughout the Peninsula) is far more likely; within Johor, both remain possible and upperside characters are needed to confirm.

features.On the hindwing underside, is the postdi: No — spot 6 displaced past midway; touching or overlapping the end-cell bar; spots 5, 6 and 7 widely out of line

r_caeca.name: Arhopala caeca
r_caeca.note: Underside hair-brown with INCOMPLETE markings: FW lacks basal and central cell spots; HW lacks various basal and discal spots; postdiscal band narrow. Male: brilliant green turning to violet-blue at the tornus of both wings (diagnostic — unique in the group); FW border a thread. Short stumpy HW tail (~2 mm).

r_borneensis.name: Arhopala borneensis
r_borneensis.note: Bornean taxon; underside pattern essentially matches [A. aurea](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=429759&preferred_place_id=6734). Underside hair-brown with complete markings; HW cell exceptionally long (more than half the wing length); FW postdiscal spots in spaces 2 and 3 wider than those above vein 4 (spot 3 especially wide); dark patch in FW space 1b not solid; HW postdiscal band only partially dislocated at vein 2. HW postdiscal spot 6 widely out of line with spots 5 and 7 — inner edge of spot 6 in line with, or even further inward (basad) than, the inner edge of spot 7; spot 6 touches or overlaps the end-cell bar. Short and stout tail at vein 2, just under 2.0 mm. Male: brassy green with strong blue suffusion at base; FW border thread throughout, widening almost imperceptibly toward tornus; HW green reaches termen narrowly in spaces 5 and 6 (unlike [A. aurea](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=429759&preferred_place_id=6734) where green does not reach termen). Female: blue with a black HW border reaching at least halfway to the end of the cell.

features.On the hindwing underside, is the postdi: No — spot 6 displaced past midway; touching or overlapping the end-cell bar; spots 5, 6 and 7 widely out of line

q_camdeo_c1.question: Is there a central spot in forewing underside space 11? (camdeo group)

question_link.text: central spot in forewing underside space 11

q_camdeo_c1.hint: [A. anarte](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1340240&preferred_place_id=6734) is one of the largest and most beautiful species in the genus, easily recognized by a central spot in FW space 11. Male: shining pale silvery blue overlaid with violet toward the margins. No other camdeo subgroup member has this spot.

choices.label: Yes — central spot in FW space 11; male shining pale silvery blue with violet margins
No — no spot in FW space 11

q_camdeo_c2.question: Is the underside ground colour extensively whitened?
q_camdeo_c2.hint: [A. dispar pendleburyi](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+dispar) is immediately distinctive for its extensively whitened underside ground colour. Male: pale blue with a thread-like border; FW cell-end also whitened. All other camdeo subgroup members have a normal brown/dark underside.

choices.label: Yes — underside extensively whitened; male pale blue, thread border, whitened FW cell-end
No — underside ground colour normal (not whitened)

q_camdeo_c3.question: Is the forewing postdiscal band incomplete, ending at or before vein 7 (lower part absent)?
q_camdeo_c3.hint: [A. belphoebe cowani](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+belphoebe) and [A. johoreana johoreana](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+johoreana) both have an incomplete FW postdiscal band. All other camdeo subgroup members have a complete (or nearly complete) band.

choices.label: Yes — FW postdiscal band incomplete, ending at vein 7
No — FW postdiscal band complete (or only partially dislocated at vein 4)

q_camdeo_incomplete.question: Is the hindwing tail completely absent (tailless)?
q_camdeo_incomplete.hint: Tail presence alone separates these two species — no upperside needed. [A. belphoebe cowani](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+belphoebe) is completely tailless; both sexes have broad dark borders and pale violet-blue upperside. [A. johoreana johoreana](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+johoreana) has a short hindwing tail and is much darker violet-blue.

choices.label: Yes — tailless (no HW tail)
No — short HW tail present
Cannot determine — tail condition unclear

q_belphoebe_cell_spots_check.question: On the hindwing underside, how large are the sub-discal spots, and how close is the row to the end-cell bar?
q_belphoebe_cell_spots_check.hint: [A. belphoebe cowani](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+belphoebe) has notably large HW sub-discal spots that sit close to, almost touching, the end-cell bar. Compare against muta-subgroup species sharing the same echelon spot arrangement: [A. moorei busa](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=780493&preferred_place_id=6734) has normal-sized spots with a clear gap from the bar; [A. tropaea](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=785409&preferred_place_id=6734) has noticeably small spots.

choices.label: Large — spots large, almost touching the end-cell bar
Normal — spots normal-sized, with a clear gap from the end-cell bar
Very small — spots noticeably small
Cannot determine — spot size/bar gap not assessable

q_camdeo_c4.question: Is a very short hindwing tail (≤2 mm) present AND are the underside spots indistinct?
q_camdeo_c4.hint: [A. hellada ozana](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=980542&preferred_place_id=6734) and [A. varro selama](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+varro) both have a very short HW tail and relatively indistinct underside spots. [A. hellada ozana](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=980542&preferred_place_id=6734): tail ~2.5 mm; FW spot 4 shifted distad (out of line with spots 5 and 6); clear violet-blue male. [A. varro selama](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+varro): tail ~1.5 mm; small species; FW spot 4 in normal position; even pale metallic silvery blue male. If this butterfly is tailless, select No.

choices.label: Yes — HW tail very short (≤2 mm); underside spots indistinct
No — no tail, or tail longer than 2 mm; underside spots distinct

q_camdeo_short_tail.question: Is the forewing postdiscal spot in space 4 shifted distad (outward, out of line with spots 5 and 6)?

question_link.text: shifted distad (outward, out of line with spots 5 and 6)

q_camdeo_short_tail.hint: [A. hellada ozana](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=980542&preferred_place_id=6734): FW spot 4 shifted distad (out of line); very short HW tail; male clear violet-blue with thread-like border. [A. varro selama](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+varro): FW spot 4 not shifted; small species; male an even pale metallic silvery blue; HW tail very short (~1.5 mm).

choices.label: Yes — FW spot 4 shifted distad; male clear violet-blue, thread border
No — FW spot 4 in normal position; small species; male even pale metallic silvery blue

q_camdeo_c5.question: Is the male a deep, dull bluish-purple with a forewing border expanding to 1.25 mm at the apex?
q_camdeo_c5.hint: [A. semperi russelli](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1148362&preferred_place_id=6734) has a distinctive deep, dull bluish-purple male (not clear violet) and a FW border that expands from thread-width to 1.25 mm at the apex, with a large cell on the underside. If the male upperside is not visible, choose 'Cannot determine' — HW spot 6 (next question) can still separate the remaining candidates, but [A. semperi russelli](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1148362&preferred_place_id=6734) may be missed.

choices.label: Yes — deep dull bluish-purple; border expands to 1.25 mm at apex; large rare species
No — clear violet-blue with narrow borders
Cannot determine — upperside not visible (note: may miss semperi russelli)

q_camdeo_c6.question: On the hindwing underside, is postdiscal spot 6 smaller than postdiscal spot 7?
q_camdeo_c6.hint: [A. camdana camdana](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+camdana): HW postdiscal spot 6 is noticeably smaller than spot 7; male clear violet-blue. [A. opalina azata](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=994269&preferred_place_id=6734): HW spot 6 is as large as or larger than spot 7; male pale violet-blue with a greyish tinge and thread-like black border. Only [A. opalina azata](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=994269&preferred_place_id=6734) is recorded in Malaysia (Langkawi, Kedah, throughout the Peninsula).

choices.label: Yes — HW spot 6 smaller than spot 7; male clear violet-blue
No — HW spot 6 not smaller than spot 7; male pale greyish-blue with violet tinge

r_anarte.name: Arhopala anarte
r_anarte.note: Tailed. One of the most beautiful and largest species in the genus. Immediately recognized by the central spot in FW underside space 11 (no other camdeo subgroup member has this). Male: shining pale silvery blue overlaid with violet toward the margins. Camdeo subgroup.

r_dispar_pendleburyi.name: Arhopala dispar pendleburyi
r_dispar_pendleburyi.note: Tailed. Long tail ~4–6 mm. Distinctive for its extensively whitened underside ground colour (diagnostic). Male: pale blue with a thread-like border; FW cell-end also whitened. Camdeo subgroup.

r_belphoebe_cowani.name: Arhopala belphoebe cowani
r_belphoebe_cowani.note: Tailless. FW postdiscal band incomplete, ending at vein 7. Underside hair-brown with markings narrowly outlined by pale buff lines; lacks a spot in the middle of HW space 8 and at the extreme base of HW space 6. Both sexes alike (monomorphic): broad dark borders (~5.0 mm at FW costa) and pale violet-blue upperside with a violet tinge. Camdeo subgroup. Very rare — no verified iNaturalist sightings; identify from this text key, not photos.

features.Do hindwing postdiscal spots 5, 6, and 7: Yes — HW spots 5, 6, and 7 in echelon (centres in straight line)
features.On the hindwing underside, how large are: Large — spots large, almost touching the end-cell bar

r_johoreana_johoreana.name: Arhopala johoreana johoreana
r_johoreana_johoreana.note: Tailed. Short tail. Similar to [A. belphoebe cowani](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+belphoebe) (incomplete FW postdiscal band) but possesses a short tail and is much darker violet-blue. Camdeo subgroup.

r_hellada_ozana.name: Arhopala hellada ozana
r_hellada_ozana.note: Tailed. Short tail ~2.5 mm. Underside spots indistinct. FW postdiscal spot in space 4 shifted distad (out of line with spots 5 and 6). Male: clear violet-blue with thread-like border. Camdeo subgroup.

r_varro_selama.name: Arhopala varro selama
r_varro_selama.note: Tailed. Small species. Very short HW tail (~1.5 mm). Male: even, pale metallic silvery blue upperside. FW spot 4 in normal position (not shifted). Camdeo subgroup.

r_semperi_russelli.name: Arhopala semperi russelli

features.On the hindwing underside, is postdiscal: Yes — HW spot 6 smaller than spot 7; male clear violet-blue

r_semperi_russelli.note:
Tailed. Long tail ~4–6 mm. Large and very rare. Male: deep, dull bluish-purple; FW border expands to 1.25 mm at the apex. Female: purple, with a distinct whitish patch at the end of the cell. FW ~28.0 mm. Underside: HW postdiscal spot 6 smaller than spot 7; central spot in space 1b characteristically elongate; lower half of the end-cell bar enlarged outwards. Camdeo subgroup.

Feature Scoring: the upperside colour/border check (q_camdeo_c5) may be unavailable, but the HW spot 6/7 size question above is answered "Yes" (spot 6 smaller than spot 7) as a routing shortcut — the key tree reaches [A. opalina azata](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=994269&preferred_place_id=6734) via "No" at that question, but A. semperi russelli genuinely shares the "spot 6 smaller" character with [A. camdana camdana](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+camdana).

r_camdana_camdana.name: Arhopala camdana camdana
r_camdana_camdana.note: Tailed. Long tail ~4–6 mm. Closely related to [A. opalina](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=994269&preferred_place_id=6734). Male: clear violet-blue. HW underside: postdiscal spot 6 is smaller than spot 7 (diagnostic vs [A. opalina](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=994269&preferred_place_id=6734)). Camdeo subgroup.

r_opalina_azata.name: Arhopala opalina azata
r_opalina_azata.note: Tailed. Long tail ~4–6 mm. FW postdiscal band complete, partially dislocated at vein 4 (spot 4 slightly shifted outward). Male: pale greyish-blue with a violet tinge, very narrow thread-like black border. Female: slightly deeper blue with broader borders. Underside: uniform grey-brown to hair brown; HW spots 5, 6 and 7 in echelon; spot 6 nearer to spot 5; spot 6 as large as or larger than spot 7. Restricted dark patch in FW space 1b bounded by a pale line; central spot in space 1b not unusually elongate (cf. [A. semperi russelli](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1148362&preferred_place_id=6734)). End-cell bar not often enlarged outwards at its lower end (cf. [A. semperi russelli](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1148362&preferred_place_id=6734)). HW cell approximately half the wing length or longer. FW 22–26 mm. Camdeo subgroup.

features.On the hindwing underside, is postdiscal: No — HW spot 6 not smaller than spot 7; male pale greyish-blue with violet tinge
features.Is there a restricted dark patch in FW s: Yes — restricted dark patch present in FW space 1b with pale bordering line (camdeo group)

q_camdeo_m1.question: Is the specimen tailed?
q_camdeo_m1.hint: [A. myrzala lammas](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=498177&preferred_place_id=6734) is tailed; male shining royal blue with narrow black borders. [A. myrzalina](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1564116&preferred_place_id=6734) is tailless; male has a broad FW border expanding from 1.5 mm at the tornus to 4.0 mm at the costa. Both have the distinctive crimson-brown underside with white striae and a HW space 6 basal spot.

choices.label: Yes — tailed; male shining royal blue, narrow borders
No — tailless; male broad FW border (1.5 mm at tornus → 4.0 mm at costa)

r_myrzala_lammas.name: Arhopala myrzala lammas
