r_myrzala_lammas.note: Moderately large, tailed species (exact tail length not recorded in key). Underside: crimson-brown with markings outlined by white striae; spot always present at base of HW space 6; central and outer postdiscal spots in space 7 conjoined at their upper ends. Male: shining royal blue with narrow black borders. Myrzala subgroup.

r_myrzalina.name: Arhopala myrzalina
r_myrzalina.note: Tailless. Underside: crimson-brown with markings outlined by white striae; spot at base of HW space 6; central and outer spots in HW space 7 conjoined at their upper ends; HW postdiscal spots 5, 6 and 7 more or less in line, spot 6 roughly level with spot 5 (cf. [A. antimuta](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=498184&preferred_place_id=6734) — hair-brown ground colour, spot 6 widely out of line and overlapping the end-cell bar). Male: broad FW border expanding from 1.5 mm at tornus to 4.0 mm at costa. Myrzala subgroup.

features.Is there a small spot at the extreme bas: Yes — small spot at extreme base of HW space 6

r_bazaloides_bazaloides.name: Arhopala bazaloides bazaloides
r_bazaloides_bazaloides.note: Tailed. Underside: purple-brown with markings outlined by white striae; extra spot in mid-space 8 on HW underside (diagnostic for subgroup); NO spot at base of HW space 6 (unlike myrzala subgroup). Male: dark violet-blue with regular 2.0 mm black borders. Bazaloides subgroup.

q_centaurus_amantes.question: Does the male have very broad black borders (3.0 mm at tornus expanding to 5.0 mm at apex), AND is the FW postdiscal spot in space 4 shifted distad (out of line)?

question_link.text: FW postdiscal spot in space 4 shifted distad (out of line)

q_centaurus_amantes.hint: [A. amantes](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+amantes) (South Myanmar) is keyed here due to similar silvery FW markings but is NOT in the centaurus group. It has the broadest borders in this section and the space 4 FW spot clearly shifted outward; it also lacks a costal spot in FW space 10. [A. centaurus](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=146903&preferred_place_id=6734) and [A. araxes](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+araxes) both have thread-like or narrow borders (≤1 mm). If the male upperside is not visible, choose Cannot determine — [A. amantes](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+amantes) is unlikely outside South Myanmar.

choices.label: Yes — borders 3.0–5.0 mm widening toward apex; FW spot 4 shifted distad; no FW space 10 costal spot (South Myanmar)
No — borders thread-like or ≤1 mm
Cannot determine — upperside not visible

q_centaurus_costal10.question: Is there a costal spot present in forewing underside space 10, above the end-cell bar?

question_link.text: forewing underside space 10

q_centaurus_costal10.hint: [A. centaurus nakula](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=146903&preferred_place_id=6734) has a costal spot in FW space 10 and a continuous FW postdiscal band. [A. amantes](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+amantes) lacks this costal spot, has the FW postdiscal spot in space 4 shifted distad (out of line with the spots above and below it), and the dark patch under the cell in space 1a extends to the postdiscal band.

No — no costal spot in FW space 10

q_centaurus_s1.question: Does the hindwing have a distinct tornal lobe?
q_centaurus_s1.hint: [A. araxes](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+araxes) has a distinct tornal lobe on the HW; male shining blue with violet toward apices; FW border increases from a thread at tornus to 1.0 mm at apex; FW ~27 mm; Sumatra to Sulawesi. [A. centaurus nakula](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=146903&preferred_place_id=6734) has a rounded HW tornus with a vestigial (very small or absent) tornal lobe; male purplish blue, thread border; FW 28–29 mm.

choices.label: Yes — distinct HW tornal lobe; male shining blue with violet toward apices; FW ~27 mm (Sumatra–Sulawesi)

Cannot determine — HW tornal lobe not clearly visible

r_centaurus_nakula.name: Arhopala centaurus nakula

features.Is there a costal spot present in forewi: Yes — costal spot present in FW space 10

r_centaurus_nakula.note:
Decisive character: FW cell spots outlined in distinctive greenish-silver lines on the underside — unique to this species in Peninsular Malaysia. FW postdiscal band continuous; a costal spot is present in FW space 10, above the end-cell bar. One of the largest Arhopala in the region (FW 28–29 mm). HW cell short (much less than half the wing); HW tailed; tornus rounded; tornal lobe vestigial or absent.

Two Peninsular subspecies: [A. c. nakula](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=146903&preferred_place_id=6734) (mainland, Langkawi, Singapore) and A. c. dixoni (Tioman Island only). Male: purplish blue, FW border reduced to a thread. Female: paler and more purple; FW border more intensely darkened over distal half of cell; moderately broad black borders.

Confirmatory: non-Peninsular relatives share the silvery FW markings but are not recorded here — [A. araxes](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+araxes) (Sumatra–Sulawesi; distinct HW tornal lobe) and [A. amantes](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+amantes) (S. Myanmar; very broad black borders 3.0–5.0 mm widening toward apex).

r_araxes.name: Arhopala araxes
r_araxes.note: FW ~27 mm. HW with a distinct tornal lobe (unlike [A. centaurus](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=146903&preferred_place_id=6734)). Male: shining blue with violet tinge toward apices; FW border a thread at tornus, increasing to 1.0 mm at apex. Female: blue. Range: Sumatra to Sulawesi region. Underside: FW cell spots outlined in greenish silver.

r_amantes.name: Arhopala amantes

features.Is there a costal spot present in forewi: No — no costal spot in FW space 10

r_amantes.note: Keyed here due to similar silvery FW cell markings, but technically outside the centaurus group. FW ~27 mm. Male: shining blue, very broad borders — 3.0 mm at tornus expanding to 5.0 mm at apex. Female: lighter blue. Underside: FW postdiscal spot in space 4 shifted distad (outwards), out of line with the spots above and below it; FW postdiscal band continuous elsewhere; lacks costal spot in FW space 10; dark patch under the cell in the upper part of space 1a extends all the way to the postdiscal band. Range: South Myanmar.

r_agaba.name: Arhopala agaba
r_agaba.note: Agaba subgroup of the cleander group — distinctive and unique. Underside pale purple-brown with dark markings; the subapical area of both wings is irrorated (sprinkled) with whitish scales, appearing noticeably paler than the rest of the wing. HW postdiscal spots 7, 6 and 5 widely out of line: the inner edge of spot 6 is in line with or inside the inner edge of spot 7, and spot 6 touches or overlaps the end-cell bar; spot 5 is almost always contiguous or amalgamated with the end-cell bar. The HW postdiscal band is only PARTIALLY dislocated at vein 2 — all other cleander group members have it completely dislocated. Tail filamentous at vein 2. Male: shining violet-blue with a black border ~1.0 mm wide. Female: brighter blue with broader black borders.

features.On the hindwing underside, is the postdi: No — spot 6 displaced past midway; touching or overlapping the end-cell bar; spots 5, 6 and 7 widely out of line
features.Is the postdiscal band placed very close: No — postdiscal band not especially close to submarginal lunules

All other cleander subgroup members have the FW band partially dislocated at vein 4.

choices.label: Yes — FW band not dislocated at vein 4 (straight/sinuous); male violet-blue, broad borders
No — FW band partially dislocated at vein 4

choices.label: Yes — HW spot 6 fills the gap; spot 6 touches or nearly touches the end-cell bar
No — gap between HW spot 6 and end-cell bar is wider than spot 6

r_cleander_aphadantas.name: Arhopala cleander aphadantas
r_cleander_aphadantas.note: FW postdiscal band NOT dislocated at vein 4 (straight or only sinuous). Male: violet-blue with broad black borders. Large species (FW > 21 mm); conspicuously projecting HW tornal lobe. HW band completely dislocated at vein 2.

r_zambra_zambra.name: Arhopala zambra zambra
r_zambra_zambra.note: FW postdiscal band partially dislocated at vein 4. FW postdiscal spot 9 as wide as or wider than spot 6. Male: typically deep blue. Large species (FW > 21 mm); conspicuously projecting HW tornal lobe.

features.On the hindwing underside, is the postdi: No — spot 6 displaced past midway; touching or overlapping the end-cell bar; spots 5, 6 and 7 widely out of line

r_athada_athada.name: Arhopala athada athada
r_athada_athada.note: FW postdiscal spot 9 narrower than spot 6, shifted basad. HW postdiscal spot 6 fills the gap between spot 5 and the end-cell bar. Large species (FW > 21 mm); conspicuously projecting HW tornal lobe.

features.On the hindwing underside, is the postdi: No — spot 6 displaced past midway; touching or overlapping the end-cell bar; spots 5, 6 and 7 widely out of line

r_silhetensis_adorea.name: Arhopala silhetensis adorea
r_silhetensis_adorea.note: Tailed. Large species — FW 23–26 mm. Conspicuously projecting HW tornal lobe. HW postdiscal band completely dislocated at vein 2. HW postdiscal spots 6 and 7 are oblique — inner edge of spot 7 is well inside outer edge of spot 6. Spot 6 is displaced from the postdiscal row toward the end-cell bar; the gap between spot 6 and the end-cell bar is wider than spot 6 itself (spot 6 does NOT reach the bar). Outer edge of spot 6 is sinuous and placed well inside the inner edge of spot 5. Gap between spot 5 and the end-cell bar wider than either spot. FW postdiscal band partially dislocated at vein 4; costal spot in space 10 usually present. Male: blue with FW black border just under 1.0 mm. Range: NE India and Myanmar south through Sundaland to the Philippines; throughout the Malay Peninsula and Singapore. (Feature Scoring: Q81 = 'Yes — roughly midway'; the key tree reaches this species via 'No — same level' as a routing shortcut to Q33.)

[A. phanda](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=940969&preferred_place_id=6734), whose spot 6 is quadrate). q_democritus_s1.question: Is the underside ground colour a dark chocolate brown with markings outlined by prominent silvery-white striae and dots?

r_atosia_malayana.name: Arhopala atosia malayana

r_lurida.name: Arhopala lurida

q_eumolphus_spot6_shape.question: Is the outer edge of hindwing postdiscal spot 6 outwardly straight?
q_eumolphus_spot6_shape.hint: This is the key underside character separating [A. overdijkinki](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1390509&preferred_place_id=6734) from [A. eumolphus](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=498188&preferred_place_id=6734) — both share hair-brown underside and spot 6 placed directly below spot 7. In [A. overdijkinki](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1390509&preferred_place_id=6734) the outer edge of spot 6 is characteristically outwardly straight. In [A. eumolphus](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=498188&preferred_place_id=6734) (and other species in the group) it is outwardly concave — the edge curves inward. If the shape is unclear, use the tail length question instead.

choices.label: Yes — outer edge of spot 6 outwardly straight
No — outer edge of spot 6 outwardly concave (curves inward)
Cannot determine — spot 6 edge shape unclear

q_eumolphus_spot6_shape.terms: "Concave" means curved inward — like the inside of a bowl or cave. "Outwardly concave" means the outer edge curves inward when viewed from outside the spot. Contrast with "straight", where the edge runs flat with no inward curve.

q_eumolphus_s1.question: How long is the hindwing tail?

FW postdiscal band weakly dislocated at vein 4; no spot at base of FW space 3. Male: shining violet-blue; black borders 1.0 mm wide. Female: may resemble female [A. aurea](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=429759&preferred_place_id=6734) but differs in narrower HW border and thinner tail.

features.On the hindwing underside, is the postdi: No — spot 6 displaced past midway; touching or overlapping the end-cell bar; spots 5, 6 and 7 widely out of line
features.What best describes the forewing postdis: HW spot 6 is outwardly sinuous; costal spot present in FW space 10 (select this even if FW spot 4 is also out of line)

r_arvina_adalitas.name: Arhopala arvina adalitas
r_arvina_adalitas.note: Tailless. FW ~18.0 mm — slightly larger than [A. antimuta antimuta](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=498184&preferred_place_id=6734) (FW ~16.0 mm). Underside ground colour hair-brown; tornal green scales present. Male: darker blue than [A. buddha](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=885913&preferred_place_id=6734); black border reduced to a thread. Female: purple-blue. HW postdiscal band only partially dislocated at vein 2 (cf. [A. antimuta antimuta](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=498184&preferred_place_id=6734), where it is completely dislocated at vein 2, though that character can sometimes be inconstant). HW postdiscal spots 5, 6 and 7 widely out of line; inner edge of spot 6 in line with or inside the inner edge of spot 7; spot 6 touches or overlaps the end-cell bar; outer edge of spot 6 outwardly sinuous or concave. Normally has a costal spot in FW space 10, above the end-cell bar. (2026-06-20 fix: the spot-6-displacement and vein-2-dislocation questions previously routed here only via false answers that contradicted these true features, which would have misidentified a truthfully-answered specimen into the muta/agelastus/agesilaus groups instead. The tree now checks for arvina adalitas's sinuous/concave spot 6 + FW space 10 costal spot right after the true 'touching/overlapping' and 'partially dislocated' answers, so the canonical path matches the true answers above.)

features.On the hindwing underside, what best des: Spot 6 touching or overlapping the end-cell bar; or spots 5, 6 and 7 widely out of line / displaced
features.Is the HW postdiscal band only partially: Yes — band only partially dislocated at vein 2 (shifted but connected)

r_vihara.name: Arhopala vihara
r_vihara.note: Tailed. FW 20–22 mm. FW underside spot 4 in line with spots 5–6 (distinguishes from eumolphus group where spot 4 is out of line); no costal spot in FW space 10. HW spot 6 rounded, more or less separated; HW band only partially dislocated at vein 2. Male: dark violet-blue; border slightly less than 1.0 mm. Female: bluer shade. Two ssp.: vihara on the peninsular mainland; hirava on Langkawi.

Consider genitalia dissection for difficult cases
Refer to specialised literature

r_muta_all_unclear.name: Muta subgroup — tropaea / moorei / muta (unresolved)
r_muta_all_unclear.note:
⚠ Cannot distinguish to species without male upperside colour and size.

[A. tropaea](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=785409&preferred_place_id=6734): notably small (FW 13.0–14.0 mm); male duller bluish-purple. Underside markings more quadrate.

[A. moorei busa](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=780493&preferred_place_id=6734): larger (FW 16.0–18.0 mm); male bright blue; FW and HW same shade of blue in all lights.

[A. muta maranda](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=780491&preferred_place_id=6734): larger (FW 17.0–19.0 mm); male bright blue; FW dark bright blue, HW slightly paler and more glistening; border slightly broader.

Inspect the male upperside to confirm species.

r_muta_s2_unclear.name: Muta subgroup — moorei or muta maranda (unresolved)
r_muta_s2_unclear.note:
⚠ Cannot distinguish to species without male upperside.

[A. moorei busa](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=780493&preferred_place_id=6734): FW and HW exactly the same shade of bright blue in all lights; female more purple with broader borders.

[A. muta maranda](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=780491&preferred_place_id=6734): FW dark bright blue, HW slightly paler and more glistening; borders slightly broader than moorei.

Inspect the male upperside in multiple light angles to confirm species.

r_camdeo_incomplete_unclear.name: Camdeo group — belphoebe or johoreana (unresolved)
r_camdeo_incomplete_unclear.note:
⚠ Cannot distinguish to species without upperside and tail inspection.

[A. belphoebe cowani](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+belphoebe): tailless; broad dark borders; pale violet-blue; both sexes alike.

[A. johoreana johoreana](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+johoreana): short tail present; much darker violet-blue.

Check hindwing for tail presence/absence to confirm species.

r_alea_s6_unclear.name: Alea subgroup — phaenops / normani / sublustris (unresolved)
r_alea_s6_unclear.note:
⚠ Cannot determine to species without male border pattern.

[A. phaenops sandakani](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=887006&preferred_place_id=6734): male border expands notably toward apex; HW spot 6 does not overlap end-cell bar.

[A. normani](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1340249&preferred_place_id=6734): male less shining, more violet than blue; borders narrow. ⚠ Requires genitalia dissection to confirm vs sublustris.

[A. sublustris ridleyi](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=475916&preferred_place_id=6734): bright glistening blue; borders under 0.5 mm. ⚠ Requires genitalia dissection to confirm vs normani.

Inspect the male upperside border width and sheen to narrow further.

features.On the hindwing underside, is the postdi: No — spot 6 displaced past midway; touching or overlapping the end-cell bar; spots 5, 6 and 7 widely out of line

r_alea_glist_unclear.name: Alea subgroup — normani or sublustris (unresolved)
r_alea_glist_unclear.note:
⚠ Cannot distinguish to species without male upperside sheen and colour.

[A. normani](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1340249&preferred_place_id=6734): male less shining, more violet than bright blue.

[A. sublustris ridleyi](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=475916&preferred_place_id=6734) (Ridley's Oakblue): male bright glistening blue; borders under 0.5 mm.

⚠ Note: both species may also require male genitalia dissection for definitive identification. Inspect male upperside sheen to differentiate.

features.On the hindwing underside, is the postdi: No — spot 6 displaced past midway; touching or overlapping the end-cell bar; spots 5, 6 and 7 widely out of line

q_tailed_corinda_apex.question: Does the forewing apex appear distinctly pointed and the termen straight (not rounded)?
q_tailed_corinda_apex.hint: [A. corinda](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=793494&preferred_place_id=6734) has an unusually distinctly pointed FW apex and straight termen — more extreme than the merely acute apex seen in groups such as eumolphus (e.g. [A. hellenore](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=883689&preferred_place_id=6734)), and quite different from the broadly rounded apex of most other Arhopala. This alternative character can help confirm corinda when FW space 1b is hidden. If the FW outline is not clear, choose Cannot determine and continue.

choices.label: Yes — FW apex distinctly pointed; termen straight
No — FW apex not as sharply pointed, nor termen as straight, as in A. corinda (may still be acute, e.g. eumolphus group, or broadly rounded)
Cannot determine — FW outline not clear

q_long_tail_echelon_check.question: Is there a restricted dark patch in FW space 1b (under the forewing cell), bounded outwardly by a pale line?
q_long_tail_echelon_check.hint: For long-tailed species (tail ≥5 mm) with HW spots 5, 6, and 7 in echelon: [A. aedias](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=498179&preferred_place_id=6734) has NO dark area under the forewing cell (characteristic of the aedias group). [A. opalina](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=994269&preferred_place_id=6734) has a restricted dark patch in FW underside space 1b bounded by a clearly defined pale bordering line — the same camdeo-group feature visible in shorter-tailed camdeo species.

choices.label: Yes — restricted dark patch present in FW space 1b with pale bordering line (camdeo group)
No — no dark patch present in FW space 1b
Cannot determine — FW space 1b obscured by hindwing

q_eumolphus_spot6_group.question: Is the outer edge of hindwing postdiscal spot 6 outwardly concave (curves inward)?
q_eumolphus_spot6_group.hint: This separates two subgroups within the hair-brown eumolphus group species. [A. horsfieldi](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=888216&preferred_place_id=6734), [A. eumolphus](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=498188&preferred_place_id=6734), and [A. hellenore](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=883689&preferred_place_id=6734) have a brassy green male and the outer edge of HW spot 6 is characteristically concave. [A. acta](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+acta) and [A. tameanga](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+tameanga) have a blue/violet male (not green) and the outer edge of spot 6 is outwardly straight.

choices.label: Yes — outer edge of spot 6 outwardly concave (green-male subgroup)
No — outer edge of spot 6 outwardly straight (blue/violet-male subgroup)
Cannot determine — spot 6 shape unclear

q_eumolphus_spot6_group.terms: "Concave" means curved inward — like the inside of a bowl or cave. "Outwardly concave" means the outer edge curves inward when viewed from outside the spot. Contrast with "straight", where the edge runs flat with no inward curve.

q_acta_vs_tameanga.question: Is the forewing termen evenly curved (rounded apex)?
q_acta_vs_tameanga.hint: [A. acta](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+acta) has a rounded FW apex with very faint underside markings and no costal spot in FW space 10. [A. tameanga](https://www.inaturalist.org/search?q=Arhopala+tameanga) has an acute FW apex (more pointed tip, termen straight or slightly concave) with clearly defined markings.

choices.label: Yes — FW apex rounded; termen evenly curved
No — FW apex acute; termen straight or slightly concave

q_agelastus_spot3_align.question: Is the hindwing underside postdiscal spot in space 3 almost in line with the spots in spaces 2 and 4?
q_agelastus_spot3_align.hint: In [A. agelastus](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1294319&preferred_place_id=6734), spot 3 sits nearly level with spots 2 and 4 — it is not displaced outward the way spots 5–7 are. In [A. wildeyana](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=1294333&preferred_place_id=6734), spot 3 tends to follow the general outward displacement of the row.

choices.label: Yes — spot 3 almost in line with spots 2 and 4
No — spot 3 displaced, not clearly in line

q_echelon_oenea_check.question: Is the forewing apex acute with the termen nearly straight between veins 2 and 4, combined with hindwing postdiscal spots 6 and 7 distinctly narrow and rectangular (rather than rounded)?
q_echelon_oenea_check.hint: [A. allata pandora](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=847055&preferred_place_id=6734) (oenea group) also shows an echelon arrangement of HW spots 5–7, but has this distinctive combination of an acute FW apex/straight termen with narrow, rectangular HW spots 6–7 — unlike [A. delta](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=896391&preferred_place_id=6734) and [A. atosia malayana](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=498181&preferred_place_id=6734), which do not share this wing-shape/spot-shape combination.

choices.label: Yes — FW apex acute, termen nearly straight; HW spots 6–7 narrow and rectangular
No — wing shape or spot shape does not match this combination

q_delta_fw_band.question: Is the forewing postdiscal band nearly completely dislocated at vein 4?
q_delta_fw_band.hint: In [A. delta](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=896391&preferred_place_id=6734), the FW postdiscal band is nearly fully broken at vein 4 — the outer portion is clearly shifted. Also check: in [A. delta](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=896391&preferred_place_id=6734) the echelon is a tight grouping of spots 5–7 in the postdiscal row, with spot 6 at roughly the same radial level as spot 5. If spot 6 is displaced midway between spot 5 and the end-cell bar (forming a wider, more spread-out echelon), the specimen is more likely [A. atosia malayana](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?verifiable=true&taxon_id=498181&preferred_place_id=6734) — choose the No option and the next question will confirm.

choices.label: Yes — FW band nearly completely dislocated at vein 4
No — FW band not dislocated at vein 4; or spot 6 displaced midway toward the end-cell bar (wider echelon)
